subsystems.deliverable: A user states intent (optionally with a design mock/prototype) and gets back a validated, human-reviewed, committed binder at .karta/binders/<slug>.json — or an ordered set of self-sufficient binders linked by `after` edges — whose work items carry contracts, oracles, touches manifests, pinned SME pack ids, shared-term declarations, and review-surface flags, so karta-deliver/build/verify can execute the work hands-off. The gate to existence is `validate_binder.py` passing plus the user's explicit "commit" verb; after commit the binder is hook-enforced read-only.

flow.step: Phase 0 `plan:ingest` — accept intent (only hard requirement), UI-format gate on any provided design file (.jsx siblings or <script type="text/babel"> block), resolve binder location (explicit → existing .karta/binders/ → default)
flow.failure_path: no intent: ask once; UI-format check fails: tell user what was found, ask for re-export; slug undeterminable: ask user

flow.step: Phase 1 `plan:survey` — Explore subagent (or sanctioned inline fallback) surveys stack, toolchain/CI commands, env command, runtime pins, architecture docs; UI annex runs 3 parallel read-only passes per ui-analysis.md when a UI surface exists
flow.failure_path: host can't spawn subagent: run inline and flag the degraded path visibly (prose); thin context: interview the user; wrong survey output: unspecified

flow.step: `plan:sme` — run `python3 skills/karta-plan/scripts/detect_stack.py <repo-root>` for the sole matching input (dependencies+languages JSON); enumerate built-in packs + .karta/sme overlay (basename identity, disabled:true = suppression); pin always-on + token-equality matches into binder `sme`
flow.enforced_by: script (detect_stack.py) for the input; prose-instruction for enumeration, matching, and pinning
flow.failure_path: unparseable manifest: detect_stack warns on stderr, JSON stays valid (scripted); match step skipped entirely: only caught later as validate_binder.py's non-fatal empty-sme warning, and only if sme ends up empty

flow.step: Phase 2 `plan:synthesize` — synthesis subagent drafts binder JSON per the embedded brief (slug freshness, plain-language title/summary, per-item contract/oracle/touches/estimate, multi-root oracle command resolution, greenfield foundation rules, oracle traceability); main thread reviews against a 7-point checklist and fixes gaps
flow.failure_path: subagent unavailable: synthesize inline, flag it, still run plan:sme (prose); checklist failures: 'fix gaps in the main thread before proceeding' (prose)

flow.step: `plan:terms` — read all contracts at once, propose shared_terms candidates (explicit verbatim-quote edges + overlapping user-facing strings); explicitly best-effort, advisory, the only cross-item-visibility point in karta
flow.failure_path: a missed term is never declared and never enforced downstream (stated in SKILL.md:183 as accepted)

flow.step: Phase 3 `plan:surface` — compute the seven boundary signals per smart-surfaced-review.md, write surface{flagged,signals} per item, record `not-computed:<signal>` for uncomputable signals; offer review-all / review-flagged / accept-as-is
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (schema permits `surface` but never requires it; validator never checks it)
flow.failure_path: unspecified — a binder with no surface fields at all validates cleanly

flow.step: Phase 4 `plan:cost` — warn on many items / several L estimates, suggest first-wave slice
flow.failure_path: none — 'educate; don't forbid'

flow.step: Phase 5 `plan:emit` — write binder(s); for a genuine staged sequence emit a set of self-sufficient binders with `after` edges (order derived, never stored; no sequence numbers in slugs)
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction for the set decision and partitioning; schema for the `after` field shape
flow.failure_path: unspecified — when/how the Phase-2 single-draft becomes multiple binders is never described

flow.step: Validate: `uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/validate_binder.py --binder <path>` per binder — schema (with injected shared_terms shape), duplicate/dangling/cyclic depends_on, same-wave touches collision, shared_terms cross-refs, opt-out summary, empty-sme + empty-touches warnings, cross-binder after graph (dangling=warn, cycle=error), archived-slug shadow warning
flow.enforced_by: script — but *invoking* it and the 'do not proceed on failure' rule are prose
flow.failure_path: exit 1 with itemized errors; prose says fix and re-validate until pass; nothing stops a session that skips the run

flow.step: Review surface — scripted plannotator probe (`shutil.which`, exit 0/1); on success offer browser annotation session, else silent in-chat editable card; ingest annotations as binder-field edits, ask back ambiguous ones, re-run validator after edits
flow.enforced_by: script for the probe only; prose-instruction for offer, session, ingest mapping, and re-validation
flow.failure_path: failed probe: skip silently, never mention plannotator (prose); unmappable annotation: ask in chat, never guess (prose)

flow.step: Commit on the explicit "commit" verb; a set commits together in one commit; binder becomes read-only
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction for the verb gate and single-commit rule; post-commit immutability enforced by hooks/scripts/guard_binder_immutability.py (PreToolUse Write|Edit|NotebookEdit, exit 2 on tracked binder/archive writes, untracked drafts pass, fails open on internal error)
flow.failure_path: premature commit without the verb: nothing blocks it; post-commit edit attempt: hook denies with corrective stderr

flow.step: Phase 6 `plan:report` — binder path, item count, topo work order, flagged items, opt-outs, first wave, sme packs; for a set, the run order as the topo sort over `after` edges (same sort karta_next.py derives live)
flow.failure_path: unspecified

flow.step: Downstream consumption of plan artifacts: deliver preflight re-runs validate_binder.py and bails on failure; post-wave check_shared_terms.py on the integration tip (FAIL reverts the wave, PENDING skips via refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/done probe); karta-verify resolves sme[] and fails closed (BLOCKED) on unresolvable packs; karta_next.py topo-sorts after edges
flow.enforced_by: script + agent fail-closed rules (owned by other subsystems, contracts authored here)
flow.failure_path: deliver bails 'no continue anyway'; shared-terms drift reverts the wave; unresolved pinned pack = BLOCKED at verify — a plan-time typo surfaces only mid-delivery

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: detect_stack.py emits the only sanctioned matching input; the model matches pack frontmatter (`always: true` or `match: [tokens]` case-insensitive equality) and pins basenames into binder `sme[]`; project overlay .karta/sme/*.md wins on basename clash; disabled:true = suppression
sme_touchpoints.gap: the match execution itself is prose — no script computes tokens→pins, so a mis-match or skipped step produces a schema-valid binder; the only tripwire is the non-fatal empty-sme warning, which is silent when sme is merely *incomplete* rather than empty

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: matched packs' do's/don'ts shape decomposition, contract wording, and oracle assertion choice ('an Angular slice's contract speaks in standalone-component/signals terms')
sme_touchpoints.gap: purely advisory prose to the subagent; nothing verifies the draft actually reflects pack guidance at plan time — enforcement waits for the safety-auditor's Review-checklist pass on the built diff

sme_touchpoints.where: detect_stack.py manifest coverage (detect_stack.py:13-16, code reads only `root / "package.json"` etc.)
sme_touchpoints.mechanism: deterministic root-only scan of package.json, pyproject.toml, requirements*.txt, go.mod, Cargo.toml, Gemfile, composer.json
sme_touchpoints.gap: a monorepo with manifests in subdirectories (the exact `frontend/` layout SKILL.md:157 plans oracle commands for) detects nothing for that subtree, so its stack packs silently never match; no manifests exist for Java/Kotlin/Swift/mobile/ML stacks the skill claims to plan

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: pinned basenames resolved at verify (.karta/sme overlay over built-ins), overlays validated by validate_packs.py, auditor fails closed (BLOCKED) with mandatory provenance line
sme_touchpoints.gap: no plan-time check that a pinned id resolves to a real pack — validate_binder.py checks only the kebab-case pattern, so a typo'd pin passes plan and commit, then BLOCKs every item mid-delivery

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: scripted warning: 'no stack packs pinned … confirm the plan:sme matching step ran'; prose says empty is legitimate only when every always-on pack is suppressed
sme_touchpoints.gap: the validator cannot distinguish 'all always-on packs suppressed' from 'step skipped' — the discriminating rule lives only in prose the session must self-apply

scripted_checks: validate_binder.py (pure stdlib, self-tested, 27+ fixtures): JSON-schema conformance incl. additionalProperties:false, required binder/title/summary/oracle fields, slug/id/sme kebab patterns, estimate enum, oracle oneOf (check-shape XOR opt_out+reason), duplicate work-item ids, dangling depends_on, dependency cycles (DFS), same-wave touches collision with glob/prefix/normalization handling and serialize/shared_resources escapes, shared_terms shape + duplicate entry ids + dangling item refs, opt-out summary printout, cross-binder `after` graph (dangling ref = warning, cycle = fatal), archived-slug shadow warning, already-archived-binder error message
validate_binder.py advisory warnings (non-fatal, printed every run): empty sme[], shared_terms entry listing an item with empty touches
detect_stack.py (pure stdlib, self-tested): deterministic dependency+language extraction from 7 manifest kinds at repo root; malformed manifests warn on stderr while stdout JSON stays valid

check_shared_terms.py (deliver-time enforcement of the plan-authored shared_terms contract): byte-identical substring scan over each landed item's touched files, gated on refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/done; FAIL exits 1, PENDING/absent exits 0

hooks/scripts/guard_binder_immutability.py (PreToolUse hook, self-tested incl. real-git fixture): denies Write/Edit/NotebookEdit to any binder tracked in HEAD (live or archive/), passes untracked drafts, fails open on internal error
scripts/check_shared_copies.py (dev-time): per-skill reference copies (binder-reference.md, smart-surfaced-review.md, sme/, …) byte-identical to skills/_shared/
skills/karta-kaizen/scripts/validate_packs.py (verify/kaizen-time): pack shape incl. frontmatter name == file basename — the identity rule plan pinning relies on
prose_only_rules: Commit gate: 'commits only on the explicit commit verb' and 'a set commits together, in one commit' — no hook or script checks either; guard_binder_immutability only protects binders already in HEAD

'Validate before commit / do not proceed on a validation failure' (SKILL.md:240,285) — the check is scripted but nothing forces the session to run it before committing; the first unskippable run is deliver's preflight

Slug freshness against surviving git state: 'taken if refs/karta/<slug>/ namespace or karta/<slug>/* tags survive in git' (SKILL.md:132) — validate_binder.py never shells to git; only the archive-file shadow case is scripted (as a warning)

Oracle floor 'compile + type-check + lint' and 'the oracle is a real CI-facing check': {"type":"unit"} with no command and no assertions is schema-valid (validator self-test uses exactly that fixture as passing)
Oracle traceability rule (assertions must trace to the contract) and 'every work item has a contract' — contract is optional in the schema (workItem requires only id/title/summary/oracle)

UI-field conditionality: SKILL.md:281 claims 'the validator will catch' UI fields on non-UI items — false; binder-schema.json allows component_map/icon_map/token_changes/design_reference on any item unconditionally

'Populate touches for every item' (SKILL.md:152) — optional in schema; an empty touches silently degrades the wave-collision check and shared-terms enforcement (warned only when the item is listed in shared_terms)
Phase 3 entirely: computing the seven signals, writing surface{}, recording not-computed:<signal> — a binder with zero surface data validates
plan:sme execution, pack enumeration, suppression handling, and the 'empty sme legitimate only under suppression' discriminator
Degrade-visibly rules for inline survey/synthesis fallback; 'running inline is not licence to skip phases'
plannotator flow: silent-skip on failed probe, annotation→field mapping, ask-don't-guess, re-validate after edits
Plain-language standard for title/summary prose; multi-root oracle command authoring rules; greenfield/lockfile-policy rules; shared-term candidate proposal; Phase 4 cost education

perf.context_cost: SKILL.md 287 lines is the always-loaded core. References the flow directs a live session to load: binder-reference.md 198 + definition-of-done.md 37 + smart-surfaced-review.md 35 + user-facing-prose.md 30 ≈ 300 lines baseline (≈590 with SKILL.md). UI-surface runs add ui-analysis.md 278 + dtcg-tokens.md 25; policy-scope runs add ci-policy.md 41 + policy-yagni.md 30; matched sme packs add ~15-60 lines each and are loaded into the main thread for synthesis. Examples (example-binder.json 57, example-sequence 88) load on consultation. Worst case a UI+policy plan carries ~1,000+ reference lines beside the conversation. The three scripts (643+247+311 lines) are executed, not loaded — the right pattern. Frontmatter pins model: opus, effort: xhigh, so the whole interview/card-editing loop — including human-latency turns — runs on the most expensive tier.
perf.agent_spawns: Typical run: 2 subagents (1 Explore survey + 1 synthesis). UI-surface run: up to 4-5 (three parallel ui-analysis Explore passes replace the one-line survey, plus synthesis). Degraded hosts: 0 (both sanctioned inline). Optional plannotator session is an external CLI process, not an agent. None of the spawns are avoidable per the spec except via the inline fallback, which the spec treats as degradation, not an optimization.
serialization_points: Phases 0→1→sme→2→3→4→5→6 are strictly sequential by design; only the three UI Explore passes are explicitly parallelizable ('run them in parallel where the host allows')
Two human-blocking waits serialize the tail: the Phase-3/Phase-5 review card (or blocking plannotator session) and the commit verb
plan:sme must wait for the full survey even though detect_stack.py needs only the repo root — the deterministic match input could exist before the survey completes
For a set, each binder is validated one-by-one and the whole set commits in one gated commit
redundant_work: The 'Project configuration' table (SKILL.md:33-45) and the Phase-1 subagent brief (SKILL.md:79-92) specify the same detection work twice — once as main-thread config resolution, once as the survey brief — inviting a session to do both

validate_binder.py main() re-globs and re-parses every sibling binder and every archive/*.json on each invocation, so validating an N-binder set costs O(N²) JSON reads (trivial in wall time, but repeated)
The binder is validated at plan Phase 5 and again unconditionally at deliver preflight — deliberate cross-session redundancy

binder-reference.md, smart-surfaced-review.md, definition-of-done.md, sme/ etc. are byte-identical copies in 4-5 skill dirs (check_shared_copies.py enforces sync); repo-size redundancy by design so each session loads only its own copy
The plannotator scratch render duplicates the full card content (every item's title/summary/contract/touches/oracle/depends_on/flags) into a markdown file the session already holds in context

dark_areas.area: SKILL.md asserts an enforcement that does not exist: UI fields on non-UI items 'is a schema error — the validator will catch it'
dark_areas.why_risky: the owner's determinism model is calibrated by claims like this; here the safety net is fictional — binder-schema.json allows component_map/icon_map/token_changes/design_reference on every work item unconditionally, so a planner that trusts the validator to catch misplacement ships them silently
dark_areas.evidence: SKILL.md:281 ('the validator will catch it') vs binder-schema.json:115-118 (plain optional arrays in workItem, no conditionality) and validate_binder.py (no UI-conditional check anywhere)

dark_areas.area: shared_terms is rejected by the published schema
dark_areas.why_risky: binder-schema.json has top-level additionalProperties:false and no shared_terms property; only validate_binder.py's runtime injection makes it legal. Any consumer validating against the schema file itself — IDE JSON-schema binding via $id, third-party tooling, a future karta script that loads the schema without the injection — flags every shared_terms binder invalid, and the two shape definitions can drift
dark_areas.evidence: binder-schema.json:7 ('additionalProperties': false, no shared_terms key) vs validate_binder.py:29-41 (_SHARED_TERMS_SCHEMA) and :133 (schema.setdefault('properties',{})['shared_terms'] = …)

dark_areas.evidence: SKILL.md:132 (refs/karta/<slug>/ and karta/<slug>/* tag rule) vs validate_binder.py (no subprocess/git import; archived-slug check reads only .karta/binders/archive/*.json at lines 620-631)
