contracts: Binder file location: .karta/binders/<slug>.json; delivered binders archived to .karta/binders/archive/<slug>.json (slug retired forever)
Binder schema: skills/karta-plan/references/binder-schema.json (draft-2020-12, additionalProperties:false) + shared_terms shape injected only inside validate_binder.py

slug names the whole git namespace downstream: integration branch karta/<slug>/integration, wave tags karta/<slug>/*, refs refs/karta/<slug>/…; item done ref refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/done is what check_shared_terms.py probes for 'landed'
Validation command contract: `uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/validate_binder.py --binder <path>` — run at plan Phase 5 (prose) and re-run at deliver Phase 0 (deliver bails on failure)

Deliver-time consistency contract: `uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py --binder <path>` on the integration tip; vocab [PASS]/[PENDING]/[FAIL] + 'SHARED TERMS: OK|DRIFT'; FAIL reverts the wave like a failed build

sme[] pins pack file basenames; identity rule name==basename enforced by skills/karta-kaizen/scripts/validate_packs.py; resolution order .karta/sme/<id>.md over built-in references/sme/<id>.md; disabled:true = suppression; consumed by karta-verify → karta-safety-auditor with fail-closed BLOCKED vocab and mandatory provenance line 'Stack-pack check: ran|skipped|blocked — pinned […]; resolved […]; items judged: n'
detect_stack.py output {"dependencies":[…],"languages":[…]} is the only sanctioned pack-matching input (case-insensitive token equality, no substrings)
surface {flagged, signals[]} per item + `not-computed:<signal>` marker; the same seven signals re-run authoritatively at build time by karta-safety-auditor (verdict PASS|VIOLATION|BLOCKED)
oracle vocab: type ∈ unit|integration|e2e|smoke|visual, optional assertions/command/cwd, XOR {opt_out:true, reason}; floor = compile+type-check+lint (prose); opt-outs reported after every run

touches[] feeds validate_binder.py's same-wave collision check, deliver's parallelism gates (skills/_shared/parallelism-gates.md), and check_shared_terms.py's scan set; serialize / shared_resources are the sanctioned escapes
env_contract {command, supports_isolation, isolation_params, cwd} and runtime_contract {runtimes[], on_unavailable:'halt'} consumed by karta-build's preflight (halt, never auto-provision)

after[] edges: the only persisted cross-binder dependency; topo order derived (never stored) identically by plan Phase 6 prose and skills/karta-status/scripts/karta_next.py; dangling=warning, cycle=error; edge to an archived slug = satisfied
Committed binders (live + archive) are read-only: hooks/scripts/guard_binder_immutability.py denies Write/Edit/NotebookEdit on any binder tracked in HEAD; untracked drafts pass
Commit verb: binder(s) commit only on the explicit user word 'commit'; a set commits in one commit (prose contract)

plannotator probe contract: `uv run python -c "import shutil,sys; sys.exit(0 if shutil.which('plannotator') else 1)"` — shared verbatim with karta-deliver's branch-review offer; silent skip on failure

Reference-copy consistency: per-skill copies of binder-reference.md / smart-surfaced-review.md / definition-of-done.md / sme/ etc. must be byte-identical to skills/_shared/ (scripts/check_shared_copies.py)
Plain-language prose standard (references/user-facing-prose.md / karta-plainlanguage) governs binder title/summary and all human-facing plan output; machine envelope and JSON exempt

subsystems.deliverable: One binder work item carried from pickup to gated, secret-scanned, marker-tagged commits on branch karta/<slug>/item-<id> inside an isolated worktree: invoked directly (single-item hatch) the worker itself merges into karta/<slug>/integration, writes refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/done, runs shared-terms/doc-gardner/kaizen/archive end-of-life, and prints a next-step footer; dispatched in a wave it stops at the committed branch with a built (or failed) ref for the orchestrator to merge. Never a PR — the user reviews and merges the integration branch.

flow.step: Phase 0 build:classify — classify request (binder item / inspection aid / bug fix / feature / CI-policy); ticketless inspection-aid mode bypasses all binder gates (worktree off default branch, lint/test/build only, no merge)
flow.failure_path: unspecified — a misclassification silently picks the wrong workflow; user correction drops old framing

flow.step: Phase 1 Gate 1 build:gate — validate binder via `uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/validate_binder.py --binder <path>` (schema, cycles, dangling depends_on); may be skipped only when the orchestrator already validated this run
flow.failure_path: bail with validator output — hard stop, no 'continue anyway'

flow.step: Phase 1 Gates 2-3 — item id exists in work_items; every depends_on has refs/karta/<slug>/item-<dep>/done; extract ITEM_ID/ORACLE/CONTRACT/RUN_MODE/SME_PACKS. RUN_MODE (wave vs single) is an explicit dispatch signal from karta-deliver, never inferred from repo state
flow.failure_path: Gate 2: bail listing available ids; Gate 3: halt with CTA naming unmet deps; missing git identity: ask once or record 'unattributed'

flow.step: Phase 2 build:sanity — verify item vs code: cited paths exist, new-file conflicts, co-owned-file additivity, citation resolution, drift vs integration tip, contract artifact exists, runtime version note
flow.failure_path: material mismatch: flag and ask; minor drift: silently adapt and note in report

flow.step: Phase 4 build:implement — worktree `git worktree add <root>/<branch> -b karta/<slug>/item-<id> karta/<slug>/integration` off the INTEGRATION tip (create integration from default branch if first item); sanitize slug-derived shell input to [a-zA-Z0-9_/-]
flow.enforced_by: git-mechanism (worktree add fails on existing branch/path) + prose
flow.failure_path: existing branch/path: stop and ask, never clobber (usually a resumable prior run)

flow.step: Always-on mutation guard (worktree-safety.md) — before every edit: git rev-parse --show-toplevel == intended worktree, branch == item branch, refuse edits on protected branches; repeat after cd/compaction/failed patch
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (no hook covers the build worker; guard_writer_confinement.py is kaizen-only)
flow.failure_path: wrong root/branch: stop and report, do not patch; unrecoverable INTENDED_ROOT: stop and ask

flow.step: 4c/4c-ter — install deps; load SME packs (.karta/sme/<id>.md overlay over references/sme/<id>.md); repo CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md wins over pack rules via KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE with repo-rule citation
flow.failure_path: pinned sme id resolves to no file: halt with CTA naming id + both paths tried (binder error, fix via karta-plan)

flow.step: 4d implement — high-impact mutation preview before auth/routing/CI/policy edits; greenfield scaffold mode (official generator only, bound to scope, re-resolve toolchain after, provision oracle-named absent checks only via official add/plugin command, commit lockfile); KARTA-DEFER markers inline; never weaken oracle
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (binder file itself is hook-protected: guard_binder_immutability.py exits 2 on Write/Edit to committed .karta/binders/*.json)
flow.failure_path: genuine oracle-or-contract conflict: halt with CTA; absent official mechanism for an oracle-named check: halt; check exists-but-fails: real failure, no carve-out

flow.step: Phase 5 build:floor — runtime preflight (compare active runtime vs runtime_contract or pin files; halt on mismatch, never install/select a runtime; tool-imposed floors: greenfield may pin a compatible TOOL version, edit mode halts); then <lint>/<typecheck>/<test>/<build> from resolved cwd through the project's own runner (no shims/synthetic roots); command-shaped oracles execute HERE
flow.failure_path: runtime mismatch: halt with CTA; floor failure: fix in-thread, surface to user after ~2 attempts; DTCG token-conformance scan (single pass) folded in when resolved

flow.step: Phase 5b build:datalayer — conditional loop ≤3 rounds: changed-files vs integration tip (git diff --diff-filter=ACMR $integration...HEAD), read-only validator in isolated subagent/worker returning `STATUS: PASS|ISSUES_FOUND` + `Issues found: N across M files`; fix, re-run lint/test, re-validate
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (report contract is prose, parsed by the LLM)
flow.failure_path: round 3 residual: surface to user (accepted residual becomes KARTA-DEFER + external follow-up); validator crashes twice: skip and note in report — never block on tooling

flow.step: Phase 6 precondition — `git diff --quiet $integration...HEAD`; empty diff = no-op item, applies even to opt-out items
flow.failure_path: halt 'produced no changes', write NO ref at all, nothing to merge; gate-side twin: empty diff handed to reviewer is BLOCKED

flow.step: 6-sme self-check (pre-gate, pre-opt-out) — run each pack's Review checklist against the diff; per miss: fix, or declare KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE(<rule-id>) with rationale [optional ceiling/upgrade]; record tally
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (teeth live downstream: undeclared miss = VIOLATION at safety-auditor)
flow.failure_path: never halts the build itself

flow.step: Phase 6 build:acceptance — opt-out skips the loop (floor still applies, reason reported); visual oracle: karta-verify boundary-only mode must PASS before dev-server work, then karta-validate ≤3 rounds (design-validation-loop.md; exit = zero critical+major); other types: karta-verify dispatches karta-acceptance-reviewer + karta-safety-auditor, read-only, fresh thin context
flow.enforced_by: prose + hook (guard_auditor_dispatch.py fail-closed: denies a karta-safety-auditor dispatch missing binder path or resolved sme checklist evidence) + schema (validate_packs.py at the verify dispatcher)
flow.failure_path: kickback loops: safety/boundary max 3 attempts then ESCALATE TO HUMAN; acceptance/contract max 2 then halt-with-CTA (failed ref; debt markers never clear the cap; ways out: fix-and-rerun, karta-plan opt_out re-plan, or wave-only human accept-waiver via orchestrator); SPEC-SUSPECT halts to failed; runtime with no subagent capability: item BLOCKED at gate, surfaced, never inline self-review

flow.step: 6-dev-a..d / Phase 7 build:teardown (visual only) — port probe (bail if occupied, never kill another process), managed background dev server with recorded handle, health-poll the actual design_reference route ~60s (auth-redirect detection: a 200 login page is not the view), teardown on every exit path stopping only recorded handles; honor and never tear down a wave-bound env
flow.failure_path: occupied port: bail and ask user; route dead after ~60s: stop handle and bail with error; blocked_auth from validator: stop loop, request session setup — do not burn a round

flow.step: 9-recheck — if ANY post-gate step mutated the diff, re-run the floor commands and the boundary scan before committing ('mandatory, not advice')
flow.failure_path: unspecified if skipped — nothing detects an unchecked post-gate mutation

flow.step: 9a secret scan — `uv run skills/karta-build/scripts/scan_secrets.py` on the staged diff before EVERY commit; fixed pattern set + entropy rule + .karta/secret-scan-allowlist; --self-test embedded
flow.enforced_by: script (deterministic exit 0/1) but invocation is prose — no hook intercepts `git commit` via Bash
flow.failure_path: hit: block commit, surface file:line:pattern, mark item failed, preserve worktree, halt; resolution = remove/rotate or reviewed allow-list entry

flow.step: 9b commit — subject marker `[karta:item-<id>] <summary>` verbatim, or `Karta-Item: item-<id>` trailer when a Conventional-Commits prefix owns the subject; one form consistently per item
flow.failure_path: unspecified — resume/status parse the marker; a malformed marker breaks tracing silently

flow.step: 9c-single (RUN_MODE=single) — rebase/merge onto current integration tip, RE-VALIDATE oracle on merged result, merge, write done ref; then check_shared_terms.py (on FAIL: rewind integration to pre-merge tip, delete done ref, stop); doc-gardner + kaizen opt-ins (.karta/doc-gardner.json / .karta/kaizen.json); archive binder to .karta/binders/archive/; print karta_next.py --footer. Accept is NOT available: cap-out/SPEC-SUSPECT writes only failed
flow.enforced_by: scripts (check_shared_terms.py, karta_next.py) inside a prose procedure; archive omission is nudged by the Stop hook (guard_delivery_stop.py complete-unarchived)
flow.failure_path: merge conflict / re-validation failure: bounded rebuild against new tip, else halt writing failed ref at item-branch tip; shared-terms FAIL: multi-step git surgery (rewind + delete ref) specified only in prose

flow.step: 9c-wave (RUN_MODE=wave) — stop at committed item branch; clean pass writes refs/.../built at tip; gate halt writes failed; NEVER write done or accepted (accepted is orchestrator-only, from a live human decision); orchestrator re-validates before merging, never trusts built
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction + Stop hook (guard_delivery_stop.py blocks a session stop once per built-but-unmerged state, then the identical stop passes — a nudge, not a wall)
flow.failure_path: halted item: committed branch + failed ref is the uniform anchor a human accept-waiver merges from

flow.step: Phase 10 build:report — ~8-line plain-language summary: terminal artifact, runtime, acceptance disposition + rounds, sme tally + overrides, KARTA-DEFER register with no-trigger flags, secret-scan status, self-assessment flagging what no gate checked; preserve worktree on halt AND success
flow.failure_path: unspecified

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: binder `sme` ids resolved against project overlay .karta/sme/*.md laid over built-in references/sme/ (8 built-ins: angular, go-htmx, go-naming, minimalism, platform-native, python-fastapi, python, vue; 19-56 lines each)
sme_touchpoints.gap: none — resolution order is stated crisply and repeated at 4c-ter

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: read each pack; hold Do/Don't/Patterns/Review-checklist as implementation guidance; halt with CTA when a pinned id resolves to no file
sme_touchpoints.gap: build-side halt is prose only; the scripted fail-closed twin exists only at the gate (guard_auditor_dispatch.py + validate_packs.py in karta-verify) — a build session that skims past a missing pack is caught late, at dispatch, not at load

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: repo CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md convention wins; deviation declared as KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE citing `repo-rule: <path>:<line-or-section>`
sme_touchpoints.gap: marker syntax (rule-id grammar, legacy form tolerance, ceiling/upgrade clause) is validated by no script in build; only karta-debt harvests and the auditor judges — a malformed override could read as undeclared

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: worker runs each pack's Review checklist on `git diff $integration...HEAD`; fix or declare per miss; tally reported; never halts
sme_touchpoints.gap: the implementer grades its own checklist in prose; the only teeth are downstream (undeclared miss = VIOLATION at karta-safety-auditor), so an opt-out item — which skips the gate entirely per SKILL.md:344 — gets self-check markers that no auditor ever judges

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: karta-verify resolves packs, validates overlays with skills/karta-kaizen/scripts/validate_packs.py (malformed = BLOCKED), hands the auditor a normalized rule-id list; guard_auditor_dispatch.py hook denies a dispatch lacking checklist evidence, fail-closed; auditor reports provenance line
sme_touchpoints.gap: none — this is the most deterministic SME path in the subsystem

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: opt-in via .karta/kaizen.json; single-item hatch invokes karta-kaizen in delivery mode over the item's diff range; wave workers never run it (orchestrator's, once)
sme_touchpoints.gap: opt-in gate is a prose file-read; a single-item run that forgets the step loses the learning signal silently — no hook or footer checks it ran

scripted_checks: validate_binder.py (Gate 1): schema validity, dependency cycles, dangling depends_on — hard exit on failure

scan_secrets.py (9a): fixed 13-pattern set + entropy assignment rule, allow-list at .karta/secret-scan-allowlist, exit 1 blocks, --self-test embedded regression fixtures (215 lines, stdlib-only, PEP 723)
check_shared_terms.py (9c-single step 5): declared shared_terms drift between done items, non-zero exit halts; PENDING items skipped by the script itself
karta_next.py --footer: read-only next-action derivation from git — same engine as karta-status, so footer and command cannot disagree

guard_binder_immutability.py (PreToolUse hook, Write|Edit|NotebookEdit): denies writes to any binder tracked in HEAD (.karta/binders/*.json incl. archive/) — the 'binder is read-only to build' rule is genuinely unskippable on hook-capable hosts; fails open on internal error

guard_auditor_dispatch.py (PreToolUse hook, Task|Agent): FAIL-CLOSED — a karta-safety-auditor dispatch must carry a binder path and, for non-empty sme[], resolved rule-id checklist evidence, else exit 2
guard_delivery_stop.py (Stop hook): blocks a session stop with built-but-no-done refs or a complete-but-unarchived binder — but only once per state ('a nudge, not a wall' per its docstring line 24)
validate_packs.py (invoked by karta-verify dispatcher): malformed sme overlay = BLOCKED, never skipped

git-mechanisms: worktree add refuses existing branch/path (backs the no-clobber rule); done/built/failed/accepted refs are durable state resume derives from; first-parent-reachability of integration is the anti-forgery discriminator for accepted refs (integration-branch.md:64)
prose_only_rules: Secret-scan INVOCATION before every commit — hooks.json matches Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Task|Stop only; `git commit` runs through Bash unguarded, so a session that forgets 9a commits without scanning (memory notes 'Bash-internals' as a known deferral)
The entire worktree mutation guard (rev-parse root check, branch check, protected-branch refusal) — no hook confines the build worker's writes; guard_writer_confinement.py covers only karta-kaizen

RUN_MODE discipline: wave worker never writes done/accepted, never touches integration — pure ref-write etiquette in prose; a worker mis-defaulting to single-item mode would merge into integration mid-wave with nothing to stop it
Kickback cap counting (safety 3, acceptance 2) — no durable counter (ref, file) records attempt N; a context compaction mid-loop loses the count
Empty-diff precondition (git diff --quiet) before the gate, and the no-ref-on-whiff rule
9-recheck: re-run floor + boundary scan after any post-gate mutation — 'mandatory, not advice', enforced by nothing
Commit-marker format ([karta:item-<id>] subject or Karta-Item trailer) that resume/status parse — never validated at commit time
Never-weaken-the-oracle and the absent-check-vs-failing-check distinction in greenfield mode — judgment calls with three paragraphs of prose fencing
Runtime preflight halt (never auto-provision) and the greenfield-adapts / edit-mode-halts split for tool-imposed floors
Gate ORDER itself (floor before acceptance; boundary-only PASS before dev-server bring-up; teardown on every exit path)
Dev-server port safety (never stop another process's server) and handle-scoped teardown
Data-layer validator report contract (STATUS: PASS|ISSUES_FOUND + 'Issues found: N across M files') — an LLM-emitted format an LLM parses; the 'MUST' is prose
Single-item shared-terms failure surgery: rewind integration to pre-merge tip + delete done ref + stop (SKILL.md:419) — delicate multi-step git state rollback with no script
3-round caps for the design-validation and data-layer loops — pseudocode the model executes mentally
Degraded-inline-review acceptance when the runtime cannot spawn subagents is 'recorded like a human accept-waiver' (verification-gate.md:15) with no specified record format, ref, or trailer

perf.context_cost: SKILL.md alone is 491 lines / roughly 34k tokens (the Read tool's 25k-token cap truncated it at line 383 = 27.2k tokens). The leanest non-UI item also loads: worktree-safety 31 + binder-reference 147 + integration-branch 74 + verification-gate 67 + smart-surfaced-review 35 + declared-debt 44 + secret-scan 50 + definition-of-done 37 + user-facing-prose 30 = 515 reference lines, ~1,006 lines (~50k tokens) before touching project code. Conditional adds: design-validation-loop 124, dtcg-tokens 57, ci-policy 53 + policy-yagni 38, sme packs 19-56 each. Bloat call-outs: the ~30-entry Gotchas section (SKILL.md:461-491) restates phase rules verbatim; merge-mode semantics are stated three times (Phase 9c, Gotchas, integration-branch.md); acceptance-cap semantics three times (Phase 6, verification-gate.md:39-46, declared-debt.md:25-30); the empty-diff rule twice (SKILL.md:335, verification-gate.md:33-35); definition-of-done.md exists byte-identical in _shared/ and karta-build/references/ (deliberate, guarded by check_shared_copies.py — only one copy loads).
perf.agent_spawns: Non-visual item, clean pass: the worker session itself (a subagent when karta-deliver dispatched it) + 1 karta-verify dispatch that internally spawns karta-acceptance-reviewer AND karta-safety-auditor = ~3 spawned contexts minimum. Worst case adds: up to 2 acceptance + 3 safety kickback re-dispatches (~5 more gate contexts), up to 3 data-layer validator subagents. Visual item: 1 boundary-only karta-verify + up to 3 karta-validate rounds each spawning browser capture/compare workers, + up to 3 more rounds for a `full` alternate theme context, + dev-server process management. Avoidable: nothing specifies re-dispatching only the failed agent on kickback rather than the whole verify wrapper; and in a wave each of N workers independently re-resolves the full Project-configuration table (no shared cache contract).
serialization_points: Gate 3 blocks pickup until every depends_on is merged (by design — scheduling constraint)
floor → datalayer loop → acceptance gate → teardown → merge is strictly sequential; the boundary-only gate must PASS before any dev-server work even starts (visual)
each datalayer round and each design-validation round re-runs <lint>+<test> before re-validating
9c-single serializes merge → oracle re-validation → shared-terms → doc-gardner → kaizen → archive → footer on the integration branch
single-item oracle re-validation on the merged tip is unconditional — it re-runs even when the integration tip never moved between gate and merge (same-SHA fast-forward case)
redundant_work: floor commands (lint/test) run at build:floor, again per datalayer round, again per dvl round, again at 9-recheck on any post-gate mutation, again at single-item merge re-validation, again by the orchestrator pre-merge in wave mode, and finally in CI — three of those are deliberate altitude re-runs, but the per-round lint/test inside the loops is pure repetition when the fix touched nothing the floor covers
binder re-read + full Project-configuration resolution repeated per item; the only skip carve-out is Gate 1 validation when the orchestrator already validated
SKILL.md restates its references: a session that loads integration-branch.md and verification-gate.md re-reads ~40% of that content already present in Phases 6/9 and Gotchas
6-sme self-check runs the same Review checklists the safety-auditor re-judges minutes later on the same diff (intentional two-pass, but double work per item)

dark_areas.area: Bundled-script paths are unresolvable from the target worktree
dark_areas.why_risky: Every scripted gate the worker must invoke is given repo-relative to the KARTA PLUGIN tree — `uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/validate_binder.py` (SKILL.md:98), `uv run skills/karta-build/scripts/scan_secrets.py` (SKILL.md:389), `check_shared_terms.py` (SKILL.md:419), `karta_next.py` (SKILL.md:428) — but Phase 4 onward the session is cd'd into the TARGET project's worktree where those paths do not exist. Hooks resolve via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}; the skill prose never mentions how a session finds the plugin root. A worker whose scan command fails with 'no such file' may improvise or silently skip the deterministic gates.
dark_areas.evidence: SKILL.md:98 vs SKILL.md:205 'All subsequent phases run from inside the worktree. Stay cd'd there until the skill finishes'; hooks.json uses "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/scripts/..." while SKILL.md has no equivalent

dark_areas.area: worktree-safety.md still speaks the pre-pivot ticket/PR dialect
dark_areas.why_risky: The doc mandated before EVERY mutation contradicts the current contract at the exact moment of use: it directs state to 'PR notes' and 'ticketing-system client state' in a system whose SKILL says 'There is no ticketing system... drop all of that machinery' (SKILL.md:107) and 'No PR — ever' (SKILL.md:463); it also invents a `.karta-build-state.json` file no other doc references.
dark_areas.evidence: worktree-safety.md:10 'Ticket-lifecycle metadata writes may target the resolved ticket file location or ticketing-system client state'; :12 'Persist the implementation INTENDED_ROOT and branch in the PR notes, a run-state note, or a small .karta-build-state.json'

dark_areas.area: design-validation-loop.md variable vocabulary does not match SKILL.md Phase-1 extraction
dark_areas.why_risky: The visual gate's per-round procedure keys on DESIGN_VIEW, DESIGN_FILE_PATH, APP_ROUTE, DESIGN_NAVIGATION, DESIGN_VALIDATION_PARAMS, COMPONENT_LIBRARY_MAP and 'the ticket' — none of which Phase 1 extracts (it caches DESIGN_REFERENCE, COMPONENT_MAP, design_facts.source). The mapping is left entirely to the live LLM, and the doc's exit conditions reference PRs three times in a no-PR system.
dark_areas.evidence: design-validation-loop.md:3 'Skip the whole loop when DESIGN_VIEW is none' vs SKILL.md:348 'Skip the karta-validate gate when design_reference is none'; :76 'addressed in PR review or follow-up tickets'; :118 'before opening the PR'; :122 'don't block the PR'

dark_areas.area: Opt-out items receive no boundary scan
dark_areas.why_risky: 6-sme claims it runs pre-opt-out 'so the markers exist when the safety-auditor judges the diff' — but an opt-out item skips the whole acceptance loop, and unlike visual oracles no boundary-only dispatch is carved out for it. The seven smart-surfaced signals (the 'real gate' per smart-surfaced-review.md:27) are never re-checked on an opt-out item's actual diff; its KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE markers are never judged.
dark_areas.evidence: SKILL.md:337 'before the opt-out check (an opt-out item still gets self-checked)' vs SKILL.md:344 'Opt-out items skip the loop' — no boundary-only carve-out parallel to the visual-oracle one at SKILL.md:348

dark_areas.area: No enforcement surface for Bash-mediated git operations
dark_areas.why_risky: Every consequential git action — commit without the secret scan, ref writes (a wave worker writing done/accepted), merging to integration, weakening a test file — flows through Bash, which no hook matches. The whole ref-write etiquette and the one-writer invariant rest on prose plus after-the-fact detection (orchestrator re-validation, first-parent check on resume, Stop-gate nudge).
dark_areas.evidence: hooks.json matchers: Write|Edit|NotebookEdit, Write, Task|Agent, Stop, SessionStart — no Bash matcher; MEMORY.md records 'Bash-internals' as an explicit deferral from the 2.21.0 hooks work

dark_areas.area: Kickback attempt counts have no durable home
dark_areas.why_risky: The differentiated caps (safety 3 / acceptance 2) are the subsystem's core escalation contract, but the count exists only in the session's context. A compaction mid-kickback, or a resumed session, cannot recover how many attempts were burned; nothing in the ref scheme (built/failed/done/accepted/in-progress) encodes attempts.
dark_areas.evidence: SKILL.md:366-367 states the caps; integration-branch.md:47-52 ref scheme carries no attempt state; worktree-safety.md's resume guidance covers only INTENDED_ROOT, not loop counters

dark_areas.area: Degraded-inline-review acceptance has no recording mechanism
dark_areas.why_risky: When the runtime cannot spawn subagents, a human may accept a 'clearly-marked degraded inline review — recorded like a human accept-waiver' — but the accept-waiver record format (Karta-Accepted trailers + accepted ref) is orchestrator-only and single-item mode explicitly has no accept. What ref/trailer records a degraded-inline acceptance, and who writes it, is unspecified.
dark_areas.evidence: verification-gate.md:15 'recorded like a human accept-waiver' vs SKILL.md:418 'Accept is not available in single-item mode' and integration-branch.md:50 'Written by the orchestrator only'

gaps.gap: Secret scan is a deterministic script wrapped in a skippable prose invocation
gaps.impact: The one security gate every commit depends on can be silently omitted: forgetting 9a (or failing to resolve the plugin-relative script path from the worktree) writes the commit unscanned, and no hook, CI shim, or Stop-gate detects the omission
gaps.evidence: SKILL.md:389 prose 'Before each commit, run the bundled scanner'; hooks.json has no matcher that could intercept `git commit`

gaps.gap: Doc drift between SKILL.md and two of its own references (worktree-safety.md, design-validation-loop.md)
gaps.impact: The safety procedure and the visual-gate procedure — both loaded at high-stakes moments — use retired ticket/PR vocabulary and unextracted variable names, forcing the live session to guess mappings and risking it acting on the stale instruction (e.g. persisting state to 'PR notes')
gaps.evidence: worktree-safety.md:10-12; design-validation-loop.md:3,20,76,84,118,122 (COMPONENT_LIBRARY_MAP at :84 vs COMPONENT_MAP at SKILL.md:117)

gaps.gap: Commit-marker format is parsed downstream but validated nowhere at write time
gaps.impact: Resume, karta-status, and the orchestrator's tracing all key on `[karta:item-<id>]` / `Karta-Item:` (SKILL.md:397 'resume and integration parse it'); a typo'd or omitted marker degrades resume silently with no gate catching it at commit
gaps.evidence: SKILL.md:391-409; no hook or script checks commit subjects (hooks.json), and the dual subject-vs-trailer form adds a second shape parsers must handle

gaps.gap: Inspection-aid mode is a full gate bypass keyed on a prose classification
gaps.impact: A request misclassified as an inspection aid skips binder validation, the oracle, the acceptance gate, the secret scan (no commit protocol is specified for it), and the sme self-check — the only protections left are the mutation preview and lint/test/build
gaps.evidence: SKILL.md:77-87: the limited flow lists 7 steps, none of which include 9a or any acceptance check

gaps.gap: Stop-gate is deliberately one-shot per state
gaps.impact: The only scripted backstop for stranded built-unmerged items or an unarchived complete binder blocks a stop exactly once, then the identical stop passes — a session that shrugs past the nudge leaves the inconsistent state anyway
gaps.evidence: hooks.json description: 'blocked once per state, then the identical stop passes'; guard_delivery_stop.py:24 'a nudge, not a wall'

gaps.gap: Single-item shared-terms failure requires unscripted destructive git surgery
gaps.impact: On a [FAIL] after the merge already landed, the worker must rewind the integration branch and delete its own done ref exactly right, in prose, with no script or self-test — the highest-risk git manipulation in the subsystem is its least automated
gaps.evidence: SKILL.md:419 'rewind the integration branch to the pre-merge tip, delete refs/karta/<slug>/item-<item-id>/done, and stop'

gaps.gap: 5b data-layer validator contract depends on an LLM emitting and an LLM parsing an exact string format
gaps.impact: The loop's exit condition ('Issues found: N across M files', per-file STATUS lines) is a MUST with no schema or script; a validator that phrases its report differently breaks the deterministic exit and the loop falls back to judgment
gaps.evidence: SKILL.md:297-301; contrast with plan/verify where validate_binder.py / validate_packs.py give the same class of contract a script

contracts: binder path .karta/binders/<slug>.json — read-only to build (hook-enforced by guard_binder_immutability.py); archive path .karta/binders/archive/<slug>.json; archive commit message `chore(karta): archive binder <slug> — delivered`

branch naming: karta/<slug>/integration (per-binder base), karta/<slug>/item-<item-id> (per-item worktree branch), inspect_<short-slug> (inspection-aid mode); slug-derived shell input sanitized to [a-zA-Z0-9_/-]

ref namespace: refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/{built,done,failed,accepted,in-progress}; worker writes built/failed (wave) or done/failed (single); accepted is orchestrator-only, written last; done ⟺ merged into integration; tags karta/<slug>/wave-<N>-base and wave-<N> are the orchestrator's

commit marker: `[karta:item-<item-id>]` verbatim in subject (canonical) OR `Karta-Item: item-<item-id>` git trailer under a Conventional-Commits prefix — one form per item; accept-waiver merge commits carry Karta-Accepted + Karta-Accept-Reason trailers

RUN_MODE dispatch signal: karta-deliver explicitly tells the worker wave mode; direct invocation defaults single-item; never inferred from repo state — decides merge ownership per integration-branch.md 'Merge ownership — two modes'

binder fields consumed: oracle (type unit|integration|e2e|smoke|visual, assertions, command, opt_out+reason), contract, depends_on, serialize/shared_resources, sme[], env_contract (command/supports_isolation/isolation_params/cwd), runtime_contract (runtimes[] name/version, on_unavailable: halt), scope.included, design_facts.source, design_reference (or literal `none`), component_map/icon_map/token_changes, shared_terms

verdict vocabulary emitted/consumed: PASS, VIOLATION, BLOCKED, DEVIATION (capped), SPEC-SUSPECT, CONFORMANT (reviewer side); dvl STATUS match|partial|mismatch|blocked_auth, APP_HEALTH OK|DEGRADED|DEGRADED_AUTH, SEVERITY critical|major|minor|cosmetic; data-layer validator `STATUS: PASS|ISSUES_FOUND` + `Issues found: N across M files`

debt markers: KARTA-DEFER(<id>): <what> — <why> — follow-up: <next> (inline only, never clears a cap); KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE(<rule-id>): <rationale> [ceiling: <bound>; upgrade: <trigger>] with repo-rule: <path>:<line> citation when repo convention overrides a pack; harvested read-only by karta-debt

scripts invoked cross-skill: skills/karta-plan/scripts/validate_binder.py (Gate 1), skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py (9c-single), skills/karta-status/scripts/karta_next.py --footer (single-mode epilogue), skills/karta-build/scripts/scan_secrets.py (9a); karta-verify invokes skills/karta-kaizen/scripts/validate_packs.py

gate dispatch: karta-verify (boundary-only mode for visual oracles), karta-validate (visual acceptance), agents karta-acceptance-reviewer + karta-safety-auditor — fresh read-only sessions; caps safety=3→human, acceptance=2→halt+failed ref

sme pack resolution: .karta/sme/<id>.md overlay over skills/karta-build/references/sme/<id>.md, project-local wins; guard_auditor_dispatch.py requires rule-id checklist evidence in auditor dispatch prompts
allow-list file .karta/secret-scan-allowlist (`path glob : pattern description` lines); opt-in files .karta/doc-gardner.json and .karta/kaizen.json ({enabled, focus})

end-of-run: single-item mode owns shared-terms check, doc-gardner, kaizen, binder archive, and the karta_next footer — all of which are the orchestrator's in wave mode; Stop-gate states 'built-unmerged' and 'complete-unarchived' encode this handoff in guard_delivery_stop.py

subsystems.deliverable: A user says "deliver this binder" and gets back one assembled, reviewable git branch — karta/<slug>/integration — containing every work item built in parallel waves and merged in dependency-safe serial order, with every outcome (built/done/failed/accepted) recorded as git refs, tags, and commit trailers so an interrupted run resumes purely from git state. Terminal state is the branch plus a plain-language report; no PR, no push — the user reviews and merges it themselves.

flow.step: Phase 0 preflight: validate the binder via `uv run --script skills/karta-plan/scripts/validate_binder.py --binder <path>` (schema, cycles, dangling depends_on, same-wave touches overlap, archived-slug shadow warning)
flow.enforced_by: script — but the invocation itself is prose-instruction (SKILL.md:19-25); nothing blocks a session that skips it
flow.failure_path: bail with the validator's output — explicitly no 'continue anyway?'

flow.step: Phase 0: capture optional backlog sink (file path or append-command) as a runtime input, not a binder field
flow.failure_path: unspecified; absent a sink, gaps surface once in the Phase-7 report

flow.step: Phase 0: single-item hatch — a one-item binder hands straight to karta-build, which then owns merge AND archive (its 9c-single)
flow.failure_path: n/a — mode branch, not a check

flow.step: Phase 0: detect leftovers (karta/<slug>/ tags + refs/karta/<slug>/ refs) and prompt the user resume-or-clear; never silently do either
flow.enforced_by: git-mechanism supplies the state; the detection and the mandatory prompt are prose-instruction (SKILL.md:31-36)
flow.failure_path: user chooses; no automated path

flow.step: Phase 1: create/locate karta/<slug>/integration in its own worktree, branched from the default branch (detected via `git remote show origin` HEAD line, else main/master)
flow.failure_path: unspecified (e.g. worktree-add collision handling lives in karta-build:163, not here)

flow.step: Wave loop Step 1: derive the ready frontier — items whose every depends_on has refs/karta/<slug>/item-<dep>/done; exclude items already done
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction over `git for-each-ref` output — no script computes the frontier (SKILL.md:55)
flow.failure_path: empty frontier with unbuilt items remaining = 'dependency bottleneck — surface it and halt'

flow.step: Wave loop Step 2 pre-dispatch: apply the five parallelism gates (dependency edge, shared resource, non-isolatable env, touches overlap, explicit serialize) to decide what serializes within the wave
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (SKILL.md:61-71; parallelism-gates.md:5-11); only the plan-time touches-overlap flag is scripted, in validate_binder.py; runtime inference 'from the item plans' is pure LLM judgment
flow.failure_path: unspecified — a missed gate silently produces merge conflicts caught later at Step 3/4

flow.step: Phase 3 env binding: start env_contract.command once per wave before dispatch; inject isolation_params per item iff supports_isolation, else serialize env-needing items; tear down once after Step 4, never on partial failure
flow.failure_path: unspecified; env leak on a mid-wave session death is unaddressed

flow.step: Wave loop Step 2 dispatch: one karta-build subagent per frontier item via 'the host's parallel primitive', each in its own worktree off the current integration tip, with an explicit wave-mode signal (RUN_MODE); serial fallback if no parallel primitive
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (SKILL.md:57; karta-build SKILL.md:116 — mode never inferred from repo state)
flow.failure_path: a halted worker writes no built marker and reports; whiff (empty diff) writes no ref at all (karta-build:335)

flow.step: Wave loop Step 2 on resume: partition the frontier by built marker — items already carrying built are NOT re-dispatched (would trip karta-build's clobber guard) but recovered through the merge queue; stale built branch (re-validation fail/conflict on moved tip) halts with CTA or clears the built ref to rebuild
flow.failure_path: halt with call to action, or manual built-ref clear

flow.step: Wave loop Step 3: barrier — wait for ALL wave builds to complete or halt before any merge; then tag karta/<slug>/wave-<N>-base on the pre-merge tip
flow.failure_path: unspecified

flow.step: Wave loop Step 3 serial merge queue: for each built-marked item FIFO by completion — (1) skip if done ref exists (resume idempotency), (2) re-validate the oracle against the current integration tip, NOT trusting the worker's verdict, (3) merge ff-or-no-ff; on conflict/re-validation failure do a 'bounded' rebuild against the new tip or halt; write refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/done → merge commit, orchestrator-only
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (SKILL.md:75-83; integration-branch.md:12-29); the only mechanical backstop is guard_delivery_stop.py, which blocks a session Stop once when built refs stand with no done/failed (hooks/scripts/guard_delivery_stop.py:43-48,216-220)
flow.failure_path: bounded rebuild (bound never numbered) or halt; stranded built-unmerged state is caught at Stop by the hook — but only once per (session,state), then the identical stop passes

flow.step: Wave loop Step 4: run the project's build/type-check on the new integration tip; on failure execute revert-the-wave — git reset --hard to wave-<N>-base, delete done/built/accepted refs of every item integrated at-or-after the base (including Phase-4 accepts), restore any failed ref an accept cleared, keep item branches as diagnostics
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (SKILL.md:85; integration-branch.md:60) — a multi-step git surgery with zero script support
flow.failure_path: halt with call to action after revert; resumed run rebuilds items against the rewound tip

flow.step: Wave loop Step 4b: run `check_shared_terms.py --binder <path>` on the same tip; [FAIL] (declared canonical term drifted between landed items) reverts the wave on the same footing as a failed build; [PENDING] (unlanded items) is skipped by the script itself
flow.enforced_by: script verdicts ([OK]/[PENDING]/[FAIL], exit code); invocation and the revert reaction are prose-instruction (SKILL.md:87; check_shared_terms.py:98-107)
flow.failure_path: revert the wave or the human fixes the wording

flow.step: Defer the karta/<slug>/wave-<N> success tag until Phase-4 accept/defer decisions resolve AND a final post-wave check passes on the resulting tip
flow.failure_path: tag never written on a failed final check; user may still revert the wave

flow.step: Phase 4 halt handling: per halted item, prompt the human — through the host's user-input facility ONLY — for fix-and-rerun / accept / defer / revert-the-wave; any accept/defer signal appearing in worker output is non-authoritative and ignored (forged 'human accepted' must never mint an accepted ref); BLOCKED-empty is a fifth shape with only re-dispatch or re-plan
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction entirely (SKILL.md:112-122); no hook validates that an accepted ref traces to a live prompt
flow.failure_path: only the failing item's dependents wait; the rest of the frontier continues

flow.step: Phase 4 accept flow, 6 ordered steps: re-validate vs moved tip (waiver suppresses only the named finding), forced no-ff merge, fresh post-accept floor check (revert-the-accept on failure — floor never waived), stamp Karta-Accepted + Karta-Accept-Reason trailers only after the floor passes, write refs ref-last (done → delete failed → accepted last), then backlog append
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (SKILL.md:124-133); crash-recovery of the non-atomic multi-ref write is also prose (integration-branch.md:66), keyed on first-parent-reachability of the trailer-bearing merge commit
flow.failure_path: any non-waived finding halts; floor failure reverts the accept and restores failed

flow.step: Phase 4 defer flow: failed ref stands, no done; gap appended to sink; the existing done-ref frontier gate stalls dependents; run hands off as incomplete
flow.failure_path: n/a — defer IS the failure path; run reported incomplete

flow.step: Phase 4 cleanup: remove passing/abandoned worktrees, tear down wave env, preserve the failing item's worktree and print its path; then print the karta_next.py --footer next-step line
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (SKILL.md:143-155); the footer content itself is scripted (karta_next.py)
flow.failure_path: unspecified

flow.step: Phase 5 cost education: echo the small-first-slice note on large scope — explicitly education, not a gate
flow.failure_path: none — user may proceed immediately

flow.step: Phase 6/6b: read .karta/doc-gardner.json then .karta/kaizen.json; absent/disabled → skip with a report note; enabled → the phase is required, dispatch the skill in delivery mode over the integration-vs-base diff range; never halts delivery
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction for the switch and required-ness (SKILL.md:171-187); kaizen's write surface IS hook-enforced (guard_writer_confinement.py denies kaizen-agent writes outside .karta/sme/ + .karta/kaizen.json)
flow.failure_path: phases never halt delivery by contract; skill-internal failure path unspecified at the deliver level

flow.step: Phase 6c binder end-of-life: all items done → git mv binder to .karta/binders/archive/<slug>.json, committed on the integration branch as 'chore(karta): archive binder <slug> — delivered'; already archived → skip (resume-idempotent); any defer/halt → leave live and say so
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction for the action (SKILL.md:191-199); two mechanical backstops: guard_delivery_stop.py blocks a Stop once on complete-unarchived (guard_delivery_stop.py:49-52,221-224), and guard_binder_immutability.py denies any Write/Edit to a committed binder live or archived (archival is a move, so unaffected)
flow.failure_path: deterministic by spec — no switch, no human decision, never halts

flow.step: Phase 7 report: waves, merged (clean-done vs accepted-done), accepted (waived assertions + human reason + trailer commit), deferred, halted (+ preserved worktree paths), backlog records, gardner/kaizen results, archive status, the integration branch as the one result; optional plannotator review offer on a successful CLI probe
flow.failure_path: n/a

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: Indirect: deliver passes the binder; each build worker resolves the binder's pinned sme[] itself (karta-build 4c-ter: project overlay .karta/sme/<id>.md over built-in references/sme/), halting on an unresolvable pinned id
sme_touchpoints.gap: deliver's dispatch prompt to karta-build is unguarded — unlike the safety-auditor dispatch (guard_auditor_dispatch.py fail-closed requires binder path + resolved checklist evidence), nothing checks a build dispatch even names the binder; the pack contract survives only because the worker's own prose re-derives it

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: karta-verify/karta-safety-auditor run the stack-pack checklist per item (verification-gate.md:31), with guard_auditor_dispatch.py enforcing fail-closed checklist handoff at the Task/Agent hook
sme_touchpoints.gap: none — this is the strongest SME enforcement point in the run

sme_touchpoints.where: Orchestrator merge-time re-validation (Step 3.2) and post-accept floor check
sme_touchpoints.mechanism: none — re-validation is oracle-and-floor only
sme_touchpoints.gap: SME rules are never re-checked at the moving tip; a merge-order interaction that violates a pack rule (e.g. two items each pack-clean in isolation combining into a violation) passes silently — only the generic build/type-check and shared_terms run on the assembled tip

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: opt-in .karta/kaizen.json → karta-kaizen mines the delivery's diff range for repeated corrections and edits packs as labeled kaizen: commits; writes hook-confined by guard_writer_confinement.py
sme_touchpoints.gap: the feedback loop only fires when the project opted in AND the delivery completed to Phase 6b; a halted/reverted run — exactly where SME lessons concentrate — produces no kaizen signal by construction

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: the human's reason is captured at the prompt and stamped as Karta-Accept-Reason trailers
sme_touchpoints.gap: an accept that waives a pack-derived finding is not routed anywhere SME-shaped — the waiver lands in git trailers and the optional backlog sink, but kaizen's mining is not specified to read Karta-Accept-Reason trailers, so repeated waivers of the same pack rule do not sharpen or challenge the pack

scripted_checks: validate_binder.py — schema, dependency cycles, dangling depends_on, same-wave touches overlap, opt-out summary, archived-slug shadow warning, and a hard error when the named binder has an archived twin (validate_binder.py:585-588); hard exit on failure
check_shared_terms.py — [OK]/[PENDING]/[FAIL] with exit code; itself skips unlanded items (done-ref aware), so PENDING can never be misread as failure

guard_binder_immutability.py (PreToolUse Write|Edit|NotebookEdit) — denies any write to a binder already in HEAD, live or archived; cannot be skipped inside the Claude Code harness; fail-open on internal error

guard_delivery_stop.py (Stop) — blocks a session stop, once per (session, findings-fingerprint), on built-unmerged (built ref with no done/failed) and complete-unarchived (all done, archive uncommitted anywhere that counts); fail-open; sentinel in <git-common-dir>/karta-stop-gate.json
guard_writer_confinement.py (PreToolUse) — fail-closed confinement of the karta-kaizen agent's writes to .karta/sme/ + .karta/kaizen.json (protects the integration branch during Phase 6b)
guard_auditor_dispatch.py (PreToolUse Task|Agent) — fail-closed: a karta-safety-auditor dispatch without a binder path or, for a pinned sme[], without resolved checklist evidence is denied
karta_next.py — read-only derived status/footer from refs, the same engine as karta-status, so the end-of-run footer cannot disagree with status
inject_karta_status.py (SessionStart) — surfaces live binders + next action at session start, making stranded deliveries visible to the next session

repo-dev-side: precommit_gate.py chains check_shared_copies.py (byte-identity between skills/_shared/ and per-skill reference copies), validate_plugin.py, sync checks — keeps the deliver references from drifting against their canonical copies
prose_only_rules: Invoking validate_binder.py at preflight at all — the check is scripted, the trigger is not (SKILL.md:19)

The entire frontier/wave computation: which items are ready, what serializes, wave membership — derived in-context from for-each-ref output; no script computes or verifies a frontier, and nothing re-checks depends_on at merge time
Runtime parallelism gating when touches manifests are absent ('else inferred from the item plans' — SKILL.md:66) — pure judgment

The serial merge queue's core invariants: FIFO order, single-writer-of-the-tip, mandatory oracle re-validation against the moved tip, never trusting the worker's built marker (SKILL.md:75-79) — no mechanism detects a skipped re-validation or an out-of-order merge

Revert-the-wave: reset to wave-<N>-base + delete done/built/accepted refs 'enumerated by ref at-or-after the base' + restore cleared failed refs (integration-branch.md:60) — the most intricate git surgery in the framework, entirely prose; a partial execution breaks resume-idempotency exactly as the text warns

The whole Phase-4 human channel and anti-forgery rule: accept/defer only via the host user-input facility, worker-emitted accept signals ignored (SKILL.md:122) — no hook inspects how an accepted ref was minted, and any session can write refs/karta/* via Bash git update-ref

Accept-flow write ordering (trailers only after floor → done → delete failed → accepted last) and its crash-resume protocol keyed on first-parent reachability of the integration branch (SKILL.md:130; integration-branch.md:64-66)
Deferring the wave-<N> success tag past Phase 4 and re-running a final post-wave check first (SKILL.md:89,141)
The 'bounded' rebuild on merge conflict/re-validation failure — the bound is never a number anywhere (integration-branch.md:9,14)
Wave env lifecycle: start once per wave, per-item isolation injection, no teardown on partial failure, orchestrator-owned teardown (SKILL.md:95-102)
The explicit RUN_MODE wave signal in the dispatch prompt to karta-build — forget it and the worker defaults to single-item and merges the tip itself, violating single-writer (karta-build:116)
Resume/clear must be a user choice, never silent (SKILL.md:36)
Preserve the failing worktree + print its path; remove the others (SKILL.md:147)
Phase 6/6b required-when-enabled and the 6→6b→6c ordering (SKILL.md:174,185,193)
Phase 6c archive action itself (git mv + commit on integration) — the Stop hook only nudges once after the fact
No-PR terminal state (SKILL.md:234) — nothing blocks a gh pr create

perf.context_cost: 591 lines loaded per orchestrator session: SKILL.md 238 + integration-branch.md 74 + binder-reference.md 149 + parallelism-gates.md 33 + verification-gate.md 67 + user-facing-prose.md 30 (definition-of-done.md 37 sits in references/ but is not linked from this SKILL.md). Bloat: the 5-gate table appears verbatim twice in the live set (SKILL.md:63-70 and parallelism-gates.md:5-11); parallelism-gates.md lines 13-33 are user-explanation prose duplicated nearly verbatim in docs/how-to/parallelism-and-review.md (docs copy is fine, but the session copy loads whether or not a user asks); the merge-ownership/single-writer rule is restated at least four times (SKILL.md Phase 2 Step 3, SKILL.md Gotchas:235, integration-branch.md:16-29, and again in karta-build); revert-the-wave semantics are stated in full three times (SKILL.md:85, SKILL.md:237, integration-branch.md:60); binder-reference.md's 149 lines include ~60 lines (runtime contract, execution context, lockfiles) that only karta-build acts on.
perf.agent_spawns: Per wave of k items: k karta-build subagents, each of which internally dispatches the gate — karta-acceptance-reviewer + karta-safety-auditor (plus karta-validate for visual oracles), with retries up to caps (acceptance 2, safety 3) — so roughly 3-4+ spawns per item before any kickback. The orchestrator's own merge-time re-validation per item is spawn-ambiguous (see dark areas): read as 're-run the gate' it costs another 1-2 fresh sessions per item per merge, and again per accept. Plus 1 doc-gardner and 1 kaizen spawn when enabled. Single-item binder: 1 build chain total, no orchestrator. Avoidable: merge-time re-validation for the first item merged in a wave (the tip has not moved from that item's base — its worker-side gate result is minutes old on an identical base), and re-validation of a recovered built item on resume when the tip equals its recorded base.
serialization_points: Step 3 barrier: no merge may start until every wave build completes or halts (SKILL.md:73) — the merge queue idles behind the slowest build even though FIFO-by-completion merging could begin as each item lands
The serial merge queue itself with per-item oracle re-validation — deliberate and named ('not free', SKILL.md:225), but it is per-item full-oracle work on the critical path
serialize: true runs the item alone — 'no parallel build mates for that slot' (SKILL.md:71), idling the rest of the frontier for that slot rather than just excluding conflicting mates
supports_isolation: false serializes every env-needing item in the wave (SKILL.md:100)
Phases 6 → 6b → 6c → 7 are strictly sequential single-agent steps after all waves finish
Serial fallback when the host has no parallel primitive (SKILL.md:57) — the whole wave degrades to sequential builds
redundant_work: An item's oracle/floor territory is exercised up to 5 times: worker floor+gate in the worktree, orchestrator merge-time re-validation, post-wave build/type-check, the final post-wave check after Phase-4 decisions (a second full check per wave whenever an accept/defer occurred), then project CI — the spec defends this as altitudes (verification-gate.md:52-61) but the wave pays two full post-wave checks whenever Phase 4 fires

Ref-state derivation is implemented twice: the orchestrator's prose-driven for-each-ref reading (preflight leftovers + frontier) and karta_next.py's scripted derivation — the orchestrator re-derives by hand what the script it later calls for the footer already computes
The gate table and revert-the-wave procedure are read multiple times per session because they are duplicated across SKILL.md body, SKILL.md Gotchas, and integration-branch.md
Each wave re-derives the full frontier from scratch (by design — cheap git reads, but it is repeated LLM parsing of ref lists every wave)
validate_binder.py runs at plan commit time and again at deliver preflight; on edit-between-waves the prose asks for a third run

dark_areas.area: Prose/hook contradiction on binder edits between waves
dark_areas.why_risky: SKILL.md Gotchas says 'You can edit the binder between waves (then re-validate it), but not while a wave is in flight' — but guard_binder_immutability.py denies ANY Write/Edit to a binder that exists in HEAD, wave in flight or not; the hook's own remedy is 're-plan with karta-plan (which writes a fresh binder file)'. A mid-delivery re-plan to a fresh slug would orphan the run's refs/tags under the old slug. The documented between-waves edit path is mechanically impossible as written.
dark_areas.evidence: SKILL.md:226 vs hooks/scripts/guard_binder_immutability.py:64-71

dark_areas.area: 'Re-validate the oracle' at merge time is operationally undefined
dark_areas.why_risky: Step 3.2 says re-validate the oracle against the current tip; the accept flow says 'Re-run the gate on the item branch vs the moved tip' (SKILL.md:126); verification-gate.md:61 says 'merge re-validates the oracle... the check passes on what actually lands' implying command re-execution. Running oracle.command in the integration worktree vs dispatching the fresh-session gate agents are wildly different in cost, determinism, and what they can catch — the spec never picks one, so different runs will do different things.
dark_areas.evidence: SKILL.md:78 ('Re-validate the oracle against the current integration tip') vs SKILL.md:126 ('Re-run the gate on the item branch vs the moved tip') vs verification-gate.md:57 ('the acceptance gate... does not execute the oracle command')

dark_areas.area: Revert-the-wave ref enumeration 'at-or-after the base' has no defined procedure
dark_areas.why_risky: Refs are pointers, not ordered events; 'enumerated by refs pointing at-or-after the base' presumably means ancestry checks of each ref target against wave-<N>-base, but the text never says how (merge-base --is-ancestor? commit dates?). done refs point at merge commits on integration (ancestry-checkable) while built/accepted point at item-branch tips that are NOT descendants of the base on the integration line — a naive ancestry enumeration misses or over-collects them. Getting it wrong breaks resume exactly as the spec warns.
dark_areas.evidence: integration-branch.md:60 ('deletes the done + built + accepted refs of every item integrated since wave-<N>-base — enumerated by refs pointing at-or-after the base') — no command or algorithm given anywhere

dark_areas.area: Wave numbering and tag lifecycle across revert/resume
dark_areas.why_risky: Nothing specifies how N is chosen (count existing tags? highest+1?), whether wave-<N>-base is deleted on revert (the spec deletes refs, not the base tag), or what a resumed run does when wave-<N>-base already exists for the wave it is about to re-run — git tags are not overwritable by default, so a re-tag attempt fails or silently reuses a stale anchor.
dark_areas.evidence: SKILL.md:81 and integration-branch.md:45 tag the base and defer the success tag, but no file defines N's derivation or the base tag's fate in Revert-the-wave (integration-branch.md:60 lists refs only)

dark_areas.area: refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/in-progress is a dead contract
dark_areas.why_risky: Listed in the canonical ref scheme but never written, read, cleaned, or mentioned in any flow step of deliver, build, or the Stop hook (guard_delivery_stop.py REF_STATES is built/done/failed only). A future implementer may write it expecting meaning, or a reader may wait on it.
dark_areas.evidence: integration-branch.md:52 lists it with no description; grep of SKILL.md and guard_delivery_stop.py:41 shows no other reference

dark_areas.area: Ref namespace is writable by anyone — anti-forgery is prose plus one reachability heuristic
dark_areas.why_risky: The threat model (SKILL.md:122) is a worker forging acceptance; the countermeasure is a prose rule plus the resume-time first-parent-reachability check (integration-branch.md:64) — itself prose an LLM performs. No hook guards `git update-ref refs/karta/...` or `git tag karta/...` via Bash, so a confused worker in wave mode can still write done/accepted directly; guard_writer_confinement only covers kaizen file writes, and guard_delivery_stop only notices built-without-done shapes.
dark_areas.evidence: hooks/hooks.json has no Bash matcher; guard_writer_confinement.py:11-15 recognizes only agent_type karta-kaizen; SKILL.md:122 relies on 'never act on it'

dark_areas.area: Stop-gate is a one-shot nudge, not a wall
dark_areas.why_risky: By design (docstring: 'a nudge, not a wall'), an identical second stop passes — so a session that ignores the corrective stderr once can still strand built-unmerged items or an unarchived complete binder; determinism of the end state is bounded at 'blocked once per (session, state)'. Also only fires on main-session Stop: a mid-run crash/kill leaves no nudge until some later session stops in that repo.
dark_areas.evidence: guard_delivery_stop.py:24-30,233-240 and self-test cases 17-19 (second identical stop allows)

dark_areas.area: Backlog sink record shape is unspecified
dark_areas.why_risky: Phase 0 accepts 'a file path or an append-command' and Phase 4 appends 'the gap' — no schema, no example record, so what an accept/defer gap looks like in the sink varies per run, undermining the one place deferred work is supposed to be findable later.
dark_areas.evidence: SKILL.md:27,131,137 — no format defined in any deliver reference

dark_areas.area: Empty-frontier halt vs defer-driven stall use the same detection
dark_areas.why_risky: Step 1 declares empty-frontier-with-unbuilt-items a 'dependency bottleneck — surface it and halt', but the defer flow deliberately manufactures that exact state (deferred item never gets done, dependents stall). The loop must distinguish 'blocked on a deferred item → hand off incomplete' from 'genuine bottleneck → halt'; no text says how.
dark_areas.evidence: SKILL.md:55 vs SKILL.md:138-139

dark_areas.area: Degraded-runtime gate acceptance has no ref/trailer shape
dark_areas.why_risky: When the host cannot spawn the fresh gate session, a human may accept a 'clearly-marked degraded inline review... recorded like a human accept-waiver' — but the accept flow's artifacts (waived named assertion, Karta-Accept-* trailers, accepted ref semantics) are all defined for gate findings, not for 'the gate never ran'; how a degraded-review acceptance is recorded in the ref scheme is unspecified.
dark_areas.evidence: verification-gate.md:15 vs the accept definition in integration-branch.md:31-37 (waiver 'suppresses only the named unmet assertion(s)')

gaps.gap: No enforcement that preflight validation ran — the binder validator is a script, but a deliver session that skips straight to Phase 1 hits no hook, no schema, nothing

contracts: Binder path: .karta/binders/<slug>.json (read-only once committed — hook-enforced); archive at .karta/binders/archive/<slug>.json; archived slugs retired forever
Branch naming: karta/<slug>/integration (single assembled result), karta/<slug>/item-<id> (per-item branches, kept as diagnostics on halt/revert)
Tag naming: karta/<slug>/wave-<N>-base (pre-merge revert anchor), karta/<slug>/wave-<N> (success tag, deferred past Phase-4 resolution)

Ref namespace: refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/{built, done, failed, accepted, in-progress} — built = worker pass signal at item tip; done = orchestrator merge commit (done ⟺ merged invariant, clean-done vs accepted-done); failed = halted-at-gate anchor; accepted = human-waived item commit, written last, orchestrator-only; in-progress listed but unused

Commit markers: [karta:item-<id>] subject marker or Karta-Item: trailer; Karta-Accepted + Karta-Accept-Reason trailers on accept merges (audit source of record; trailer implies the floor passed); archive commit message 'chore(karta): archive binder <slug> — delivered'; 'docs: gardner <slug>' commits; 'kaizen:' labeled commits

RUN_MODE dispatch signal to karta-build: explicit wave-mode flag in the dispatch, never inferred from repo state; decides merge ownership (worker merges + archives in single-item hatch; orchestrator merges in a wave)

Verdict vocabulary consumed from the gate: CONFORMANT / DEVIATION (cap 2) / SPEC-SUSPECT / VIOLATION (safety, cap 3) / BLOCKED (incl. BLOCKED-empty, not accept/defer-eligible); script verdicts [OK]/[PENDING]/[FAIL] from check_shared_terms.py

Script entrypoints deliver invokes: skills/karta-plan/scripts/validate_binder.py --binder, skills/karta-plan/scripts/check_shared_terms.py --binder, skills/karta-status/scripts/karta_next.py --footer --binder <slug>

Binder fields deliver consumes: work_items[].{id, depends_on, serialize, shared_resources, touches, oracle, estimate}, env_contract.{command, supports_isolation, isolation_params, cwd}, slug, scope.included
Opt-in switch files: .karta/doc-gardner.json and .karta/kaizen.json ({enabled, focus}); absent/false = off, true = phase required
Stop-gate sentinel: <git-common-dir>/karta-stop-gate.json, session→fingerprint map, max 20 sessions
Backlog sink: optional runtime input (file path or append-command), append-only, format unspecified
Human channel: host user-input facility (AskUserQuestion idiom) is the only authoritative accept/defer source; worker-emitted signals non-authoritative
Terminal-state contract: no PR ever (no gh/glab/tea); user reviews and merges karta/<slug>/integration; plannotator probe via shutil.which('plannotator') for the optional review surface
Default-branch detection: git remote show origin HEAD line, else main/master (shared with guard_delivery_stop.py's _default_branch_revs)

Hook contract (hooks/hooks.json): payload JSON on stdin; exit 0 allows, exit 2 blocks (PreToolUse/Stop) or corrects (PostToolUse); binder-immutability + delivery-stop guards fail open, auditor-dispatch + writer-confinement fail closed on recognized shapes

skills/_shared/ canonical copies byte-identical to per-skill references (integration-branch.md, parallelism-gates.md, verification-gate.md, definition-of-done.md, user-facing-prose.md), enforced by scripts/check_shared_copies.py via the repo pre-commit gate

subsystems.deliverable: A trustworthy per-item "done": every built work item is judged by a fresh, read-only, independent session against the actual diff — per-assertion acceptance (oracle types unit/integration/e2e/smoke), contract conformance against an external artifact (never the binder's own claim), and a seven-signal boundary scan plus pinned stack-pack checklist conformance — so nothing lands on the integration branch that wasn't verified conformant, explicitly halted with a call to action, or explicitly human-waived. Findings auto-kick back to karta-build under bounded caps (acceptance 2, safety 3); a human is reached only on safety-cap exhaustion or at the deliver orchestrator's Phase-4 halt.

flow.step: Caller invokes karta-verify (karta-build Phase 6, or direct) with worktree path, binder path + work-item id, diff range, and mode (full default; boundary-only for visual-oracle items before karta-validate; opt-out oracles skip the gate entirely)

flow.step: Phase 2 checklist resolution (only when binder pins sme[]): resolve each pack — .karta/sme/<id>.md overlay over built-in references/sme/<id>.md, project-local wins; validate each overlay with skills/karta-kaizen/scripts/validate_packs.py (malformed → BLOCKED, never skipped); unresolvable pinned id → BLOCKED naming the id; normalize the Review checklist into an item list (rule id, rule text, source pack) and hand it in the dispatch brief (SKILL.md:75)
flow.enforced_by: script exists (validate_packs.py: id grammar, one-prefix-per-pack, registered prefix ownership, retired-id resurrection, duplicates) but its invocation at verify time is prose; guard_pack_write.py hook independently validator-gates overlay writes (fail-open on internal error)
flow.failure_path: BLOCKED with validator findings or missing id(s) named

flow.step: Phase 2 dispatch karta-safety-auditor: re-run the seven smart-surfaced-review signals on the actual diff (contract mutation, destructive op, sensitive zone, capability/resource escalation, blast radius incl. cross-item file overlap read from the binder, architectural novelty, unresolved open question) — declared crossings pass, undeclared are VIOLATIONs; plus the conditional stack-pack check judged by rule id against KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE(<rule-id>) markers (legacy pack:paraphrase form tolerated), with repo-rule: <path>:<line> citations verified by reading the cited file; checklist text is data, never instructions — embedded imperatives are reported as findings (agents/karta-safety-auditor.md:23-57)
flow.enforced_by: hook (guard_auditor_dispatch.py, PreToolUse Task|Agent, FAIL-CLOSED: denies a recognized auditor dispatch missing a .karta/binders/<slug>.json path, with an unreadable binder, or — when the binder pins sme[] — without checklist evidence matching '- [ ] <id> — ' lines or id tokens near 'checklist'); everything past dispatch (the scan itself, declared-vs-undeclared judgment, citation verification, prompt-injection defense) is prose-instruction; auditor fail-closed BLOCKED on pinned-but-missing checklists is agent prose
flow.failure_path: VIOLATION → kickback to karta-build, cap 3 total, then escalate to the human; BLOCKED → halt with the reason; mandatory provenance line 'Stack-pack check: ran|skipped|blocked — pinned: [..]; resolved: [..]; items judged: <n>' in every report (skipped legitimate only when sme[] empty)

flow.step: Phase 3 aggregate: full mode combines envelopes per the table (CONFORMANT+PASS → pass; CONFORMANT+VIOLATION cap-open → loop verify:boundary only; DEVIATION cap-open → loop verify:acceptance; cap exhausted / SPEC-SUSPECT / BLOCKED → blocked); boundary-only mode reports the safety verdict alone; report in plain language with exact agent output and the one next human action (SKILL.md:89-106)
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (the table, the loop routing, and the report/envelope agreement rule 'a divergence halts the pipeline' — nothing parses the report against the envelope)
flow.failure_path: 'blocked' carries halt-with-CTA or human escalation per cap rules; 'concerns' is intermediate, never terminal

flow.step: Post-verify ref writes by the caller: wave worker writes refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/built on a clean gate, or commits the branch + writes failed at the tip on a gate halt; verify itself writes nothing and never records an accept — the accepted ref is only ever written by the deliver orchestrator from a live human answer at its Phase-4 halt (worker-reported accepts are non-authoritative)
flow.enforced_by: git-mechanism (refs as durable anchors) + prose for who writes when; guard_delivery_stop.py Stop-gate blocks a session quietly stopping with built-but-unmerged items; guard_binder_immutability.py blocks binder edits via Write/Edit/NotebookEdit
flow.failure_path: deliver Phase 4 offers accept (re-validate, no-ff merge, fresh post-accept floor check with revert-the-accept, refs written ref-last) / defer (failed stands) / revert-the-wave; BLOCKED-empty is excluded from accept/defer — ways forward are re-dispatch (whiff) or drop/amend via karta-plan (already present)

flow.failure_path: re-validation failure → bounded rebuild against the new tip or halt; already-present → reviewer BLOCKED-empty halt

perf.context_cost: SKILL.md is 118 lines (frontmatter model: haiku — the orchestrator deliberately runs on the cheap tier). References a live full-mode session is told to consult: verification-gate.md 67, binder-reference.md 147, definition-of-done.md 37, smart-surfaced-review.md 35, user-facing-prose.md 30 — ~434 lines worst case, plus each pinned sme pack at Phase 2. The Codex-plugin fallback additionally injects the bundled agent bodies as dispatch prompts: karta-acceptance-reviewer.agent.md 150 + karta-safety-auditor.agent.md 123. Each agent session loads its own 159/132-line instruction file plus binder JSON plus the diff. Bloat: binder-reference.md is 147 lines of which the bulk is runtime_contract/env-command execution detail the read-only verify orchestrator never uses (and it omits the one field Phase 2 needs, sme); smart-surfaced-review.md's seven signals are duplicated verbatim inside the auditor body (deliberate self-containment, but the skill still links the reference too, so a careful orchestrator loads the same signals twice).
perf.agent_spawns: Full mode: minimum 2 subagent spawns per work item (acceptance-reviewer then safety-auditor), both pinned model: opus / effort: high — the expensive tier — while the dispatching skill is haiku. Worst case 5 gate spawns (2 acceptance attempts + 3 safety attempts), each kickback also re-entering the karta-build worker for a fix round. Boundary-only mode: 1 spawn. Visual items pay boundary-only here plus karta-validate afterwards. In a wave, deliver Step 3 re-validates each item's oracle against the moved tip — per the empty-diff design doc (line 42) by dispatching the reviewer directly — so every wave item pays the acceptance reviewer at least twice (build-time gate + merge-time re-validation). Avoidable: the sequential acceptance→boundary ordering means the second opus spawn waits on the first even though the two verdicts, caps, and inputs are independent; and each safety re-dispatch re-resolves and re-hands the full checklist set (no caching is permitted by the no-stored-state doctrine).
serialization_points: SKILL.md:87 mandates the two agents run sequentially — verify:boundary does not start until verify:acceptance clears or exhausts its cap, though the gates are independent (different verdicts, different caps, no data dependency)
Each kickback round serializes: agent verdict → karta-build fix → re-dispatch agent on the corrected diff (up to 2+3 rounds)
Boundary-only must PASS before any dev-server work on visual items (deliberate early-halt, but it serializes gate before env bring-up)
Wave merge is serial FIFO with per-item oracle re-validation against the moving tip (deliberate single-writer design; each merge waits on the previous item's re-validation)
redundant_work: karta-build's 6-sme self-check runs the same Review checklists over the same diff the safety-auditor then re-judges — a deliberate two-layer (marker-producing vs judging) but a full duplicate pass per item

The floor runs in the build worktree (karta-build build:floor), then verify Phase 0.3 'checks the floor' again before dispatch — with no stated mechanism for how a read-only skill checks it other than re-running or re-deriving it

The oracle command executes at the build floor, is re-validated at merge against the moved tip, and runs again in CI — documented as deliberate altitudes (verification-gate.md:54-61), but it is three executions of one command per item

Each gate agent independently re-reads the binder and re-runs git diff every attempt (no-stored-state doctrine: 'the disposition is re-derived by reading on each run'), and every safety re-dispatch re-receives the full normalized checklist set
The seven signals text exists in three loaded forms: _shared/smart-surfaced-review.md, its karta-verify/references copy, and embedded verbatim in the auditor agent body

dark_areas.why_risky: The empty-diff design's Layer-2 catch for the already-present case only works if deliver Step 3 dispatches the acceptance reviewer; if the orchestrator instead re-executes the oracle command, an already-present item's empty diff is never classified BLOCKED and the catch silently doesn't exist
dark_areas.evidence: docs/specs/2026-06-17-empty-diff-verdict-design.md:42 says Step 3 works by 'dispatching the reviewer directly with no Phase-6 wrapper around it', but skills/karta-deliver/references/integration-branch.md:14 says 're-run the item oracle on the merged result' and karta-deliver SKILL.md Step 3.2 says only 'Re-validate the oracle against the current integration tip'

A visual-oracle whiff that slips past karta-build's prose-only git diff --quiet guard reaches boundary-only mode, where an empty diff trivially fires no signal — PASS — and the item proceeds toward karta-validate as if delivered
dark_areas.evidence: agents/karta-safety-auditor.md:71 (BLOCKED = missing/unreadable inputs only, no empty-diff clause); the design doc's Files-to-change table (docs/specs/2026-06-17:81) patched only karta-acceptance-reviewer

dark_areas.why_risky: Aggregate row 'CONFORMANT + VIOLATION (cap not exhausted) → loop verify:boundary' re-runs only Phase 2 on the corrected diff; the fix that cleared the boundary finding changed the diff the CONFORMANT verdict was issued against, and nothing re-runs Phase 1. karta-build's own post-gate-mutation rule mandates re-running the floor and the boundary scan but not acceptance
dark_areas.evidence: skills/karta-verify/SKILL.md:96 (loop verify:boundary only) and skills/karta-build/SKILL.md:387 ('re-run the definition-of-done floor (build:floor commands) and the boundary scan on the mutated diff' — acceptance absent)

The hooks resolve it via CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT (guard_pack_write.py:34), but the skill prose never tells the session how to locate the plugin root, so the mandatory overlay validation may fail to run and an LLM may 'helpfully' skip it — exactly the silent-skip the BLOCKED rule exists to prevent

perf.context_cost: Live karta-validate session loads SKILL.md (209 lines) + its three referenced files: user-facing-prose.md (30), verification-gate.md (67), definition-of-done.md (37) = 343 lines. Bloat: verification-gate.md is mostly karta-verify/auditor machinery (caps, SPEC-SUSPECT, stack-pack handoff, empty-diff BLOCKED) irrelevant to a read-only visual comparison, and definition-of-done.md is the build-floor doctrine — validate needs perhaps 10 of those 104 reference lines. The invoking build session additionally loads design-validation-loop.md (124 lines) per visual item. The two Python scripts (314+146 lines) are executed, not loaded — good separation.
perf.agent_spawns: One karta-validate invocation: 1 skill session + 0-2 workers (capture worker, comparison worker — each 'subagent OR host worker', so both can be inline). One visual build item worst case: 1 karta-verify boundary-only dispatch + 3 validate rounds x (1 session + up to 2 workers) ≈ 10 spawns. Avoidable: the capture worker adds a spawn around a fully deterministic script that needs no judgment — the skill session could run capture_view.py directly and spawn only the comparison worker (the one that genuinely needs fresh, thin context).
