subsystems.deliverable: At any moment the user gets one truthful "you are here + single next action" answer recomputed fresh from git (never stored): by default the live Karta Watch browser page (serve_status.py, 2.6s poll), with a one-shot terminal map / --json / --footer (karta_next.py) as headless fallback and a <=10-line SessionStart context injection (inject_karta_status.py hook). karta-debt separately hands the user a one-shot read-only ledger of every KARTA-DEFER / KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE marker so deliberate shortcuts stay visible instead of rotting.

flow.step: Trigger ('what's next', 'karta status', 'watch') loads skills/karta-status/SKILL.md; session decides watch-page (default) vs one-shot text
flow.failure_path: unspecified — nothing forces the browser-default; a session can silently pick the terminal path

flow.step: Launch serve_status.py --root <repo> --port 8765 as a persistent managed background process, confirm it is serving, hand the user the working URL
flow.failure_path: SKILL.md:39-41 warns a bare `&`/nohup is often reaped before binding; beyond that warning, launch failure handling is unspecified

flow.step: Per HTTP request, current_state(): load_binders() + load_archived_binders() + gather_git_facts() + derive_state() + _enrich/_append_archived; serve / (full HTML rebuild) and /state.json
flow.failure_path: corrupt binder JSON silently skipped (karta_next.py:236-237); git OSError yields '' facts so everything derives not_started with no error surfaced (karta_next.py:213-218); junk archive rows degrade to empty merged rows by design (serve_status.py:94-96)

flow.step: Browser polls /state.json every 2600ms; on fetch failure flips to 'reconnecting… — read-only' amber state and keeps last state
flow.failure_path: reconnecting banner; no retry backoff; file:// snapshots never poll

flow.step: State derivation: topo sort over `after` edges (dangling=warning, cycle=error+order null, archived predecessor=satisfied, shadowed archived slug=warning); binder status merged/in_flight/not_started; item frontier done/failed/built/building/ready/blocked with precedence done>failed>built>branch>deps
flow.failure_path: cycle: errors list + order null + 'blocked' next_action; dangling after: warning row; both rendered at top of terminal map and carried in JSON

flow.step: _next_action ladder: (1) in_flight w/ failed item -> 'karta-deliver <slug>' fix/re-plan; (2) in_flight w/ building|ready|blocked -> resume; (3) first is_next not_started -> start; (4) all merged -> done; (5) fallback 'no binder is ready to run — check the warnings/errors above'
flow.failure_path: built-only and all-done-awaiting-merge states fall to rule 5 with EMPTY warnings/errors — misleading dead-end message (empirically confirmed)

flow.step: SessionStart: hooks.json invokes inject_karta_status.py; it shells karta_next.py --json (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT resolution, 15s timeout) and prints <=10 lines (per-binder status + packs + next action), silent when no binders
flow.failure_path: fail-open and silent: any exception, engine crash, or timeout degrades to static binder summary or nothing, always exit 0 (inject_karta_status.py:57-70,177-187)

flow.step: Run-end footer: karta-deliver and single-item karta-build are told to run `karta_next.py --footer --binder <slug>` so the printed next step and the engine never disagree
flow.failure_path: unspecified — skippable; wave workers explicitly must NOT print it (build SKILL.md:430)

flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction only (karta-debt/SKILL.md:17-31 — no script exists in the skill)
flow.failure_path: unspecified; empty result has a specified message ('No karta debt markers. Clean ledger.')

perf.context_cost: Live-session load is small: karta-status/SKILL.md = 59 lines, karta-debt/SKILL.md = 35 lines. The 395-line engine and 1075-line server never enter context (invoked via uv run). SessionStart injection is capped at 10 lines (inject_karta_status.py MAX_LINES). Both skills are pinned `model: haiku` in frontmatter. No bloat.
perf.agent_spawns: 0 subagents per use for both skills — everything runs inline in the invoking session plus OS subprocesses (uv/python/git). The watch page costs one long-lived managed background process per watch session. karta-debt is one grep plus inline formatting.
serialization_points: gather_git_facts is sequential subprocess spawning: per binder 1 for-each-ref + 1 rev-parse, per item 1 rev-parse (branch probe) + 1 merge-base (when done) — O(2B+2I) git processes per derivation, re-paid on every watch poll (each connected client, every 2.6s) and every '/' page load
the ThreadingHTTPServer runs derivations concurrently per request, but each derivation is itself a serial chain of git calls
redundant_work: the same full state derivation is recomputed independently by: each watch poll, each '/' page render, the SessionStart hook, and the run-end footer step in deliver/build — by design ('derived fresh, never stored'), but the per-item branch rev-parse could be folded into one for-each-ref over refs/heads/karta/<slug>/

render_app_html rebuilds the entire HTML document (CSS + _build_app_js string substitutions over the 350-line app template) on every '/' request even though only the inlined state changes; assets are the only cached responses (86400s)

guard_delivery_stop.py carries an independent second implementation of ref-state reading (its own for-each-ref parse + built/done/failed set logic, hooks/scripts/guard_delivery_stop.py:114-118,216-223) parallel to gather_git_facts — two codebases answering 'what state is item X in'

subsystems.subsystem: sme (stack packs: 8 built-ins, pack schema/validator, binder pinning, plan/build/verify injection, override markers, kaizen loop, house packs, hooks, benchmarks)
subsystems.deliverable: A karta user gets domain-expert guardrails applied to every build without configuring anything: packs auto-matched from repo manifests are pinned into the binder, their advisory Do/Don't/Patterns shape how code is written, and their Review checklist is enforced at the safety-auditor gate — an unfixed, undeclared miss kicks the item back to build. Deliberate deviations survive as auditable KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE markers, and (opt-in) kaizen migrates pack ownership into the repo as human-reviewed commits.

flow.step: Author a pack: built-in in skills/_shared/sme/*.md (7 packs + platform-native.md reference), or project overlay in .karta/sme/*.md; overlay wins on basename clash; disabled:true = suppression pack
flow.enforced_by: script (validate_packs.py exists) but its invocation for built-ins is prose-instruction only (stack-packs.md:81-87 'Run the bundled validator'); .karta/sme/ writes through Claude Code tools are hook-enforced (guard_pack_write.py)
flow.failure_path: hook path: PreToolUse Write denied exit 2 with findings; PostToolUse Edit/Write exit 2 feeds findings back to model. Manual/built-in path: unspecified — nothing runs the validator on skills/_shared/sme/

flow.step: Mirror sync: skills/_shared/sme/ copied byte-identical into skills/{karta-plan,karta-build,karta-verify}/references/sme/
flow.failure_path: check exits 1 listing drifted/missing files; but only when someone runs it — no CI wiring visible in this subsystem's files

flow.step: plan:sme step 1 — run detect_stack.py <repo-root>; emits {dependencies, languages} from root-only manifests; this JSON is the only matching input
flow.enforced_by: script output, prose-instruction to run it (karta-plan/SKILL.md:106)
flow.failure_path: unparseable manifests warn on stderr and are skipped; JSON stays valid. Skipping the script entirely: only backstop is validate_binder's non-fatal empty-sme warning

flow.step: plan:sme steps 2-4 — enumerate overlays over built-ins, skip suppressed and platform-native, match tokens case-insensitively whole-token, pin basenames into binder sme[]
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (karta-plan/SKILL.md:107-111); LLM performs the equality matching; no script computes the pinned set
flow.failure_path: validate_binder.py fails the binder if sme is not an array of ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$ ids (binder-schema.json:48-51); empty sme is a warning, never a failure (validate_binder.py:261-270)

flow.step: plan Phase 2 — matched packs feed synthesis as domain guidance (contracts/oracles respect pack patterns); never a plan-time gate
flow.failure_path: unspecified — purely advisory

flow.step: build 4c-ter — resolve each pinned id (.karta/sme/<id>.md else references/sme/<id>.md), load Do/Don't/Patterns/checklist as implementation guidance; missing id halts the item
flow.failure_path: halt with call to action naming each missing id and both paths tried; karta-verify treats the same gap as BLOCKED

flow.step: build 6-sme — pre-gate self-check of every checklist item against git diff integration...HEAD; each miss is fixed or declared via inline KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE(<rule-id>): rationale [ceiling; upgrade]; per-pack tally into build report; never halts
flow.failure_path: skipping it is caught downstream only: an undeclared miss becomes a VIOLATION at the safety-auditor gate

flow.step: verify:boundary dispatch — read binder sme[], resolve packs, validate each project overlay with validate_packs.py (malformed = BLOCKED, halt), normalize checklists into rule-id/text/pack item list, hand to safety-auditor
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (karta-verify/SKILL.md:75) invoking a script; backstopped by hook guard_auditor_dispatch.py which denies a karta-safety-auditor dispatch whose prompt lacks a binder path or checklist evidence when sme[] is non-empty (fail-closed)
flow.failure_path: BLOCKED report with validator findings or missing ids; hook denies dispatch exit 2

flow.step: safety-auditor judges diff against checklist items only (never advisory prose); marker matched by rule id (legacy pack-name paraphrase 'plausibly the same rule' tolerated); pack text treated as data, imperatives inside packs reported as findings; mandatory provenance line in every report
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction in agents/karta-safety-auditor.md:37-57,99-105; the pass/miss judgment itself is LLM judgment, no script
flow.failure_path: undeclared miss = VIOLATION → kickback to build, max 3 attempts then escalate to human; pinned-but-unresolved = BLOCKED, never skipped

flow.step: deliver:kaizen / build single-item hatch step 7 — read .karta/kaizen.json; absent/false = skip with one-line note; true = required, dispatch karta-kaizen writer in delivery mode over the run's diff range
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (karta-deliver/SKILL.md:180-187, karta-build/SKILL.md:422); config shape schema-checked by validate_plugin.py:250-267 and kaizen-schema.json when committed
flow.failure_path: never halts the delivery; direct invocation with switch off = report off and stop (prose only)

flow.step: kaizen agent — first enabled run seeds every used pack into .karta/sme/ as full files (existing project copy wins); beyond seeding, phase 1 edits only on a concrete dispatched instruction; never weakens a rule, never promotes to enforcing; returns envelope {seeded, packs_changed, residual, summary}
flow.enforced_by: write surface hook-enforced (guard_writer_confinement.py: only .karta/sme/ + .karta/kaizen.json, fail-closed for recognized agent_type karta-kaizen); pack validity hook-enforced (guard_pack_write.py); 'never weaken' and envelope shape are prose only (agents/karta-kaizen.md:16-22,55-60)
flow.failure_path: out-of-surface write denied exit 2; invalid pack content denied/fed back; a weakened-but-syntactically-valid rule has no scripted catch — human review of the kaizen: commit is the only backstop

flow.step: kaizen land — pre-land run of validate_packs.py over every changed .karta/sme/ file; delivery mode commits 'kaizen: <summary>' on the integration branch (never push); direct mode leaves working-tree edits
flow.enforced_by: prose-instruction (karta-kaizen/SKILL.md:58-61) doubled by the guard_pack_write hook at write time on Claude Code
flow.failure_path: validator findings returned to agent once to fix; second failure lands nothing and the validator output is the phase result

flow.step: karta-debt harvest — grep KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE markers repo-wide into a one-shot read-only ledger; legacy-format markers flagged for migration
flow.failure_path: unspecified — read-only, writes nothing

flow.step: benchmark — manual A/B: build fixture with packs pinned vs sme:[]; run.py compares added LOC + manifest dep pins; safety axis via acceptance gate on both arms; honesty rule bans per-repo savings claims
flow.enforced_by: script for the diff comparison (benchmarks/sme/run.py); the protocol, arm capture, and honesty rule are prose (benchmarks/sme/README.md)
flow.failure_path: unspecified — the one recorded run substituted static diff-reading for the protocol's karta-verify behavioral gate and disclosed it (results/2026-07-06-date-picker.md:11-14)

perf.context_cost: Built-in packs total 268 lines across 8 files (angular 33, go-htmx 34, go-naming 38, minimalism 27, platform-native 56, python-fastapi 27, python 19, vue 34). Plan must enumerate all of them plus overlays (~268+ lines) once per planning run; each build item loads only pinned packs (typical vue repo: minimalism 27 + vue 34 + platform-native 56 via see_also ≈ 117 lines); verify re-loads the same packs per item and additionally ships the normalized checklist inside the auditor dispatch prompt. karta-kaizen SKILL.md is 74 lines and the agent file 72. Off-session weight (not loaded live): validate_packs.py 339, stack-packs.md 89, benchmarks ~191. Bloat callouts: platform-native.md (56 lines) is pulled by see_also from 5 of 7 packs, and every built-in pack exists in 4 identical in-repo copies (canonical + 3 skill mirrors) — a maintenance cost, not a token cost.
perf.agent_spawns: Plan: 0 SME-specific spawns (matching is inline). Build: 0 (6-sme self-check is inline; the safety-auditor spawn exists regardless of SME — SME only fattens its dispatch prompt). Verify: 0 extra. Kaizen when enabled: +1 writer spawn per delivery AND per single-item karta-build hatch (karta-build/SKILL.md:422); post-seed this spawn is provably inert — phase 1 gives the agent 'no signal to act on by yourself' (agents/karta-kaizen.md:40) and the dispatcher never constructs edit instructions — so it is an avoidable spawn on every delivery until phase 2 ships. Model split: kaizen skill frontmatter pins haiku for orchestration, sonnet/effort-high for the (inert) writer.
serialization_points: 6-sme self-check must complete before any gate dispatch (ordering mandated so markers exist for the auditor) — serializes marker production ahead of the gate per item
verify dispatcher must resolve + validate + normalize all pinned packs before the auditor dispatch (and guard_auditor_dispatch denies otherwise) — repeated serially for every item verified
deliver:kaizen runs after doc-gardner and before binder EOL, serially, once per delivery
guard_pack_write runs the validator as a synchronous subprocess on every .karta/sme write; a Write is validated twice (PreToolUse on content + PostToolUse on disk)
redundant_work: The same pinned pack files are re-read at plan (guidance), at every build item (4c-ter), and at every verify dispatch (checklist extraction) — N items × 2-3 reads of identical content, never cached or embedded in the binder

Every checklist rule is judged twice per item against the same diff: build 6-sme self-check, then the safety-auditor's independent judgment — intentional (marker production vs verdict) but doubles the LLM checklist work
validate_packs.py re-runs on unchanged overlays at every verify dispatch of every item
4 byte-identical copies of each built-in pack maintained in-repo (skills/_shared/sme + 3 references/sme mirrors), plus a 5th seeded copy in consuming repos after kaizen's first run
kaizen delivery-mode re-resolves the pack set the binder already pins (cheap, but a re-derivation the skill itself forbids in the other direction)

dark_areas.area: Built-in packs are never machine-validated
dark_areas.why_risky: The subsystem's whole enforcement chain starts from pack files, yet nothing automated ever runs validate_packs.py on skills/_shared/sme/*.md or the references mirrors — guard_pack_write's PACK_RE matches only '.karta/sme/*.md' paths, and validate_plugin.py's only pack check is the minimalism shadow byte-compare. A malformed edit to a canonical built-in ships, and karta-verify validates only project overlays ('Before extracting anything from a project overlay, validate it'), so a broken built-in checklist reaches the auditor unparsed.
dark_areas.evidence: hooks/scripts/guard_pack_write.py:28 (PACK_RE = r"(?:^|/)\.karta/sme/.+\.md$"); scripts/validate_plugin.py has no validate_packs invocation (grep hits only the shadow check at :94-110); karta-verify/SKILL.md:75 scopes validation to 'a project overlay'

dark_areas.area: Enforcement teeth end at format; verdicts are pure LLM judgment
dark_areas.why_risky: The owner wants determinism, but every consequential decision — does this diff violate min.2, does this marker declare that crossing, did the repo convention outrank the rule — is an LLM reading prose. Scripts guarantee only that the checklist parsed and that the auditor was handed it. Two different auditor runs can disagree on the same diff with no scripted tiebreak.
dark_areas.evidence: agents/karta-safety-auditor.md:39 ('judge the diff against those checklist items') and :49 (legacy marker declared when its paraphrase 'plausibly names the same rule')

dark_areas.area: All hook enforcement is Claude-Code-only
dark_areas.why_risky: On Codex (or any runtime without the plugin hooks) kaizen writer confinement, pack-write validity, and fail-closed auditor dispatch all degrade to prose. The agent file admits it: 'On Claude Code this is enforcement, not doctrine alone.' The kaizen path even documents a Codex fallback that spawns a generic write-capable worker with only the agent md as instructions — no confinement hook applies to it.
dark_areas.evidence: agents/karta-kaizen.md:26 ('On Claude Code this is enforcement'); karta-kaizen/SKILL.md:37 (Codex plugin install 'cannot register subagents' → fresh write-capable subagent)

dark_areas.area: Monorepo blindness in matching
dark_areas.why_risky: detect_stack.py reads manifests at the repo root only; a workspace with packages/*/package.json or services/*/pyproject.toml detects nothing below root, so packs silently fail to pin. The only backstop is the advisory empty-sme warning, which does not fire when sme is non-empty-but-partial (e.g. minimalism pinned, vue missed).
dark_areas.evidence: skills/karta-plan/scripts/detect_stack.py:13 ('Manifests read (repo root only)') and code paths root/'package.json' etc.; validate_binder.py:267 warns only when binder.get('sme') is falsy

dark_areas.area: Kaizen seed freezes packs against upstream improvements
dark_areas.why_risky: After the first enabled run, .karta/sme/ copies 'are the packs' and built-ins 'cover only names the repo does not carry' — so plugin releases that fix or sharpen a built-in rule (e.g. the go-pack review fixes in 4fbd308) never reach a seeded project. Only the karta repo itself has a drift guard, and only for one pack (minimalism shadow byte-identity).
dark_areas.evidence: agents/karta-kaizen.md:36 ('From then on those files are the packs'); scripts/validate_plugin.py:98-110 covers exactly .karta/sme/minimalism.md

dark_areas.area: Kaizen 'never weaken' has no mechanical floor
dark_areas.why_risky: guard_pack_write checks syntax, guard_writer_confinement checks paths; neither compares old vs new semantics. A kaizen (or prompt-injected) edit that rewrites min.1's rule text to be vacuous while keeping the id and format passes both hooks and lands as a commit — human review of a labeled commit on an integration branch is the entire defense, and deliver:kaizen 'never halts the delivery'.
dark_areas.evidence: agents/karta-kaizen.md:18 (prose rule); guard_pack_write.py validates format only; karta-deliver/SKILL.md:187 ('fully automatic, no human, no halt')

dark_areas.area: Benchmark evidence for the whole subsystem is one fixture, n=3, statically judged
dark_areas.why_risky: The claim that packs 'earn their place' rests on a single date-picker fixture where both arms independently chose the native input (the pack's trap never bit), acceptance was judged by reading diffs rather than the protocol's karta-verify gate, and run.py measures only added-LOC and manifest dep pins — no fixture exercises angular/vue/python/go checklist rules at all, and automation is blocked on a headless build path.
dark_areas.evidence: benchmarks/sme/results/2026-07-06-date-picker.md:11-14 (static-judging caveat) and :76-79; benchmarks/sme/README.md 'Automation note'; benchmarks/sme/fixtures/ contains only date-picker.md

dark_areas.area: Unregistered-prefix collision across project packs is unchecked
dark_areas.why_risky: validate_packs.py checks each file independently: an unregistered pack must not squat a registered prefix and must be internally consistent, but two different project packs can both claim prefix 'house' (or any unregistered token). KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE(house.1) then ambiguously names two rules, and the auditor's by-rule-id matching has no disambiguation rule.
dark_areas.evidence: validate_packs.py:114-127 — prefix ownership map covers only PREFIXES; no cross-file state between validated packs

gaps.gap: No script computes the pinned pack set from detect_stack output + pack frontmatter — the deterministic matching rule is executed by the planning LLM
gaps.impact: The subsystem's entry point (which rules apply at all) is the least deterministic step in it; a skipped or fumbled match under-pins packs with only a fully-empty-sme warning as backstop
gaps.evidence: karta-plan/SKILL.md:106-111 (steps 2-4 are prose); detect_stack.py stops at emitting JSON

gaps.gap: Plan-time enumeration of project overlays does not validate them; only verify does
gaps.impact: A hand-committed malformed overlay (written outside Claude Code tools, so no hook fired) sails through planning, gets pinned, and the failure surfaces as BLOCKED at verify — items deep into a delivery, the most expensive place to discover a syntax error
gaps.evidence: karta-plan/SKILL.md:107 mentions validate_packs.py only as the mechanism enforcing name==basename; the explicit validate-then-BLOCK instruction exists only in karta-verify/SKILL.md:75

gaps.gap: Edit-based pack writes are validated only post-hoc; a malformed pack persists on disk if the session dies before repairing
gaps.impact: guard_pack_write deliberately passes PreToolUse Edit ('an Edit delta has no full content to validate'); the exit-2 PostToolUse feedback assumes a live model to act on it — an interrupted session leaves a pack that will BLOCK the next verify
gaps.evidence: guard_pack_write.py:62-64 and self-test case 'PreToolUse Edit passes (PostToolUse covers it)'

gaps.gap: guard_auditor_dispatch checks that checklist-shaped text is in the prompt, not that it is the right checklist
gaps.impact: A dispatch carrying stale, truncated, or wrong-pack rule ids passes the hook (ITEM_LINE_RE / ID_TOKEN_RE are shape checks); completeness relies on the auditor's own prose fail-closed rule of comparing against the binder's pinned ids
gaps.evidence: guard_auditor_dispatch.py:33-45 (_has_checklist_evidence matches any '- [ ] x.1 — ' line or any id token after the word 'checklist')

gaps.gap: The kaizen envelope (seeded/packs_changed/residual/summary) has no schema or validator
gaps.impact: deliver:kaizen folds the envelope into the report and derives the commit message from it; a malformed envelope degrades silently into a wrong or empty kaizen: commit body
gaps.evidence: agents/karta-kaizen.md:55-60 defines it as a YAML block in prose; kaizen-schema.json covers only the opt-in config file

gaps.gap: go-htmx's own vendoring mandate defeats its own auto-matching, patched by a documented manual escape
gaps.impact: The pack most likely to apply to a Go+htmx repo (vendored htmx, stdlib templates) never auto-pins; the user must know to copy the pack to .karta/sme/go-htmx.md with always:true — documented in two places but executed by nobody automatically
gaps.evidence: skills/_shared/sme/go-htmx.md Patterns bullet ('vendored htmx leaves no manifest trace'); docs/how-to/stack-packs.md:38

gaps.gap: 6-sme self-check compliance is unverifiable downstream
gaps.impact: The report line ('stack-pack self-check (angular): 4/4 ok') is free text; nothing cross-checks the tally against the auditor's independent count of items judged, so a fabricated or skipped self-check is indistinguishable from a real one whenever the auditor also passes
gaps.evidence: karta-build/SKILL.md:342 ('This self-check never halts the build — it produces the markers and the report line')

gaps.gap: Two of the three house-pack rules duplicate existing scripted checks but are enforced only as LLM checklist judgments
gaps.impact: house.1 (mirrors regenerated) is exactly check_shared_copies.py's check and house.2 (--self-test present) is mechanically greppable, yet at the gate both are judged by the auditor reading the diff — redundant LLM work where a script already exists, and weaker than running the script
gaps.evidence: .karta/sme/karta-house-skill-authoring.md checklist vs scripts/check_shared_copies.py

gaps.gap: validate_binder does not check pinned sme ids against any known pack set
gaps.impact: A typo'd id ('minimlism') passes the slug pattern, plans clean, then halts every build item at 4c-ter / BLOCKs verify — the earliest scripted point that could catch it (plan-time validation) only shape-checks
gaps.evidence: binder-schema.json:48-51 (pattern only); karta-build/SKILL.md:171 is where the typo finally halts

contracts: binder field `sme`: array of pack ids matching ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$, id == pack file basename sans .md (binder-schema.json:48; validate_packs.py enforces name==basename)

pack resolution order everywhere: project overlay .karta/sme/<id>.md wins over built-in references/sme/<id>.md; suppression pack = frontmatter disabled:true, enumerated but never pinned; platform-native.md is reference data, never a pack

pack file grammar: frontmatter keys exactly name/description/match/always/see_also/disabled; exactly one of match|always unless disabled; fixed heading '## Review checklist'; item line '- [ ] <prefix>.<n> — <text>'; tombstone '- ~~<prefix>.<n>~~ retired: <reason>'; ids never recycled
registered prefix registry (validate_packs.py PREFIXES): minimalism=min, angular=ng, vue=vue, python-fastapi=fapi, python=py, go-htmx=htmx, go-naming=goname; 3500-byte pack size warning threshold

override marker grammar: KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE(<rule-id>): <rationale> optionally [ceiling: <bound>; upgrade: <trigger>]; legacy form KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE(<pack>: <paraphrase>): tolerated, karta-debt flags 'legacy-format (migrate to rule id)'; repo-convention override cites repo-rule: <path>:<line-or-section>

verdict vocab: PASS | VIOLATION | BLOCKED (safety-auditor); mandatory provenance line 'Stack-pack check: ran|skipped|blocked — pinned: [<ids>]; resolved: [<ids>]; items judged: <n>'; skipped legitimate only when binder pins no sme[]; kickback cap max 3 then human

detect_stack.py output {"dependencies": [...], "languages": [...]} is the ONLY matching input; match = case-insensitive whole-token equality; languages vocabulary: python, javascript, node, go, rust, ruby, php

kaizen config: .karta/kaizen.json {enabled: bool required, focus: string optional} (kaizen-schema.json; validate_plugin.py:250-267); switch absolute — absent/false means never runs, direct invocation included

kaizen commit label: 'kaizen: <short summary>' on the integration branch, seed form 'kaizen: seed <n> packs into .karta/sme/'; never pushed; kaizen writable surface exactly .karta/sme/ + .karta/kaizen.json (guard_writer_confinement.py, agent_type karta-kaizen bare or *:namespaced)
kaizen envelope: seeded[], packs_changed[], residual[], summary (prose contract, no schema)

canonical→mirror: skills/_shared/sme/ is source; skills/{karta-plan,karta-build,karta-verify}/references/sme/ must be byte-identical and complete (check_shared_copies.py); this repo's .karta/sme/minimalism.md is a managed shadow byte-identical to the canonical (validate_plugin.py:_check_kaizen_shadow)

hook contract: payload JSON on stdin; exit 0 allows, exit 2 blocks (PreToolUse) or feeds corrective stderr to the model (PostToolUse); guard_pack_write fail-open, guard_writer_confinement and guard_auditor_dispatch fail-closed on their recognized shapes

build/deliver integration: 6-sme runs pre-gate pre-opt-out on git diff <integration>...HEAD; deliver:kaizen runs after doc-gardner over the same blast-radius diff range; single-item hatch runs kaizen too, wave workers never do
benchmark honesty rule: report only A/B deltas on fixtures, never per-repo savings; the only honest per-repo figure is the karta-debt ledger

subsystems.subsystem: hooks (enforcement layer: 6 plugin hook guards + hooks.json wiring, repo precommit gate, mirror-sync machinery, validate_plugin, skills-lock)
subsystems.deliverable: A karta user gets deterministic, below-the-agent enforcement of the rules that matter most: committed binders cannot be edited, stack packs cannot land malformed, the safety-auditor cannot be dispatched without its binder and SME checklists, the kaizen writer cannot write outside .karta/sme/ + .karta/kaizen.json, a session cannot silently stop with a dirty delivery, and (repo-only) no commit lands with drifted mirrors or broken plugin integrity. Plus a free session-start binder summary. All of it runs as harness-invoked stdlib scripts — no agent judgment, no context load.

flow.step: Plugin install: Claude Code loads hooks/hooks.json (convention path at plugin root); ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} resolves each script. validate_plugin._check_hooks guards that every referenced script exists, is chmod +x, and that no script in hooks/scripts/ is orphaned (unreferenced = never runs)
flow.failure_path: validate_plugin exits 1, which blocks commit via the repo gate; at runtime a missing/non-exec script just never fires (harness-dependent, unspecified)

flow.step: SessionStart: inject_karta_status.py prints ≤10 lines (one per live binder: slug, items, packs, next action) by shelling to skills/karta-status/scripts/karta_next.py --json (15s timeout), degrading to a static summary; silent when no binders
flow.failure_path: always exit 0; any exception is swallowed silently (inject_karta_status.py:185-187) — a broken status engine degrades or vanishes with no signal

flow.step: PreToolUse Write|Edit|NotebookEdit: guard_binder_immutability denies (exit 2) writes to .karta/binders/*.json or archive/*.json that exist as blobs in HEAD (git ls-tree); untracked drafts pass
flow.failure_path: FAIL-OPEN: any internal error (git missing, bad payload) exits 0 and the write proceeds (main():157-161)

flow.step: PreToolUse Write on .karta/sme/*.md: guard_pack_write validates the proposed content in a temp file via skills/karta-kaizen/scripts/validate_packs.py (resolved via CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT, fallback to own plugin root); failure denies with findings
flow.failure_path: FAIL-OPEN: validator unresolvable or internal error exits 0 (line 58-59, 186-187). PreToolUse Edit is deliberately unchecked (line 63-64)

flow.step: PostToolUse Edit|Write on .karta/sme/*.md: guard_pack_write re-validates the file now on disk; exit 2 feeds findings to the model as corrective feedback
flow.failure_path: corrective only — the invalid pack stays on disk; nothing reverts it if the model ignores the stderr feedback. Missing file passes

flow.step: PreToolUse Task|Agent: guard_auditor_dispatch recognizes a karta-safety-auditor dispatch (substring match on subagent_type/agent_type/agent/agent_name/name), then requires a .karta/binders/<slug>.json path in prompt+description, a JSON-loadable binder, and — when binder sme[] is non-empty — checklist evidence ('- [ ] <prefix>.<n> — ' item lines OR any rule-id token after the word 'checklist')
flow.failure_path: FAIL-CLOSED on recognized shape: missing path/unreadable binder/missing evidence AND internal errors all deny with re-dispatch instructions (main():169-183); unrecognized shapes always pass

flow.step: PreToolUse Write|Edit|NotebookEdit: guard_writer_confinement recognizes payload top-level agent_type == karta-kaizen (bare or *:namespaced, exact); every file_path/notebook_path must normpath-resolve inside a .karta/sme/ segment or exactly .karta/kaizen.json; no extractable path = denied as unverifiable
flow.failure_path: FAIL-CLOSED for the recognized writer including internal errors (main():154-166); everything else passes

flow.step: Stop: guard_delivery_stop derives live slugs (working tree ∪ HEAD, never archive/), reads refs/karta/<slug>/item-*/{built,done,failed} via for-each-ref, and blocks (exit 2) on built-unmerged (built with no done/failed) or complete-unarchived (all done, archive/<slug>.json not committed in HEAD, refs/heads/karta/<slug>/integration, or the default branch). Block-once: findings fingerprint recorded per session in <git-common-dir>/karta-stop-gate.json (pruned to 20 sessions, atomic write); identical repeat stop passes
flow.failure_path: FAIL-OPEN: non-Stop/subagent-shaped/stop_hook_active payloads, missing fields, non-repo cwd, malformed binder, unresolvable sentinel all exit 0 silently; a 'failed' ref is a designed resting state, never a finding

flow.step: Repo-only PreToolUse Bash (.claude/settings.json, 600s timeout): precommit_gate.py detects 'git commit' via word-boundary regex over segments split on &&/||/;/|/newline, then runs 5 gates fail-fast from repo root, 100s each: check_shared_copies, sync_codex_skills --check, sync_codex_agents --check, validate_plugin, validate_packs over skills/_shared/sme/*.md (minus platform-native.md) + .karta/sme/*.md. First failure blocks with gate name + last 40 output lines
flow.enforced_by: script (precommit_gate.py) via project settings hook — requires one-time user approval, NOT shipped in plugin
flow.failure_path: FAIL-OPEN on internal errors; KARTA_SKIP_GATE=1 in command text or env skips everything (line 116); a timed-out gate is a block, not a stall

flow.step: Sync write mode (manual, dev-run): sync_codex_skills mirrors skills/*/ into .agents/skills/ and plugins/karta/skills/ + .codex-plugin into plugins/karta/.codex-plugin as byte-identical real directories (no symlinks, Windows: openai/codex#8400); deletes orphans incl. rmtree of whole non-canonical skill dirs, preserving only skills with COMPLETE skills-lock.json entries. sync_codex_agents projects agents/*.md into .codex/agents/<name>.toml (pinned codex_model + effort ∈ {low,medium,high,xhigh}, sandbox derived from tools) and skills/<BUNDLE_SITE>/references/<name>.agent.md
flow.enforced_by: script, but RUNNING it is prose — only the --check side is gated
flow.failure_path: write-mode errors raise; --check drift blocks commit via gate; commits made outside a Claude Code session in this repo never run any check (no CI)

flow.step: validate_plugin (gate 4): SKILL.md frontmatter + cited-path existence, agents frontmatter, marketplace.json exact skill enumeration both directions, Claude/Codex plugin.json name+version parity, Codex marketplace shape, full re-verification of both skill projections and agent projections (imports the sync modules), per-skill openai.yaml, doc-gardner/kaizen config shape, minimalism.md shadow byte-identity, hook manifest + exec bits + all 6 --self-tests executed live
flow.failure_path: exit 1 lists all findings; blocks commit only through the precommit gate or a human running it

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: denies a safety-auditor dispatch when the binder pins sme[] but the prompt carries no resolved Review-checklist evidence — the deterministic backstop for karta-verify's prose rule that checklists must travel (karta-verify/SKILL.md:75)
sme_touchpoints.gap: presence-only: _has_checklist_evidence (guard_auditor_dispatch.py:41-45) accepts ANY '- [ ] x.1 — ' line or any id token after the word 'checklist' — it never checks the ids belong to the pinned packs, cover all pinned packs, or match the packs' current rules; a stale, truncated, or wrong-pack checklist passes the guard

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: chains to validate_packs.py so packs land only validator-clean; the kaizen pre-land syntax check enforced below the agent
sme_touchpoints.gap: PreToolUse covers only Write — an Edit lands first and is validated post-hoc as corrective feedback the model can ignore, leaving a malformed pack on disk (guard_pack_write.py:63-64, 83-93); a pack silently dropping out of matching is exactly the failure mode the message warns about

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: confines the kaizen SME writer to .karta/sme/ + .karta/kaizen.json, fail-closed, keyed on the verified top-level agent_type payload field
sme_touchpoints.gap: none for its declared scope (kaizen's tools are Read/Glob/Grep/Edit/Write/Skill per agents/karta-kaizen.md frontmatter — no Bash to shell around it)

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: every commit validates skills/_shared/sme/*.md and .karta/sme/*.md syntax, and byte-compares the seeded .karta/sme/minimalism.md shadow to canonical
sme_touchpoints.gap: the shadow byte-identity check is hard-coded to minimalism.md only (validate_plugin.py:104-106); any other built-in pack seeded into .karta/sme (the go packs shipped in 2.21.0 would qualify) could drift from canonical with only syntax checked. platform-native.md is exempted by filename string match only (precommit_gate.py:68-69)

sme_touchpoints.mechanism: surfaces each binder's pinned packs ('packs: minimalism') in the session-start summary — passive SME visibility
sme_touchpoints.gap: informational only, and the whole line vanishes silently on any engine failure

scripted_checks: Committed-binder immutability vs HEAD via git ls-tree (guard_binder_immutability.py) — covers live + archive/, Write|Edit|NotebookEdit
Pack syntax on every Write (pre, denying) and Edit/Write (post, corrective) under .karta/sme/ via validate_packs.py subprocess
Safety-auditor dispatch completeness: binder path present + binder loads + checklist evidence when sme[] non-empty, FAIL-CLOSED including on internal error
Kaizen writer path confinement, FAIL-CLOSED including unverifiable-target denial
Delivery Stop-gate: built-unmerged and complete-unarchived detection from git refs only (never transcript), block-once-per-(session,fingerprint) via git-common-dir sentinel
Repo commit gate: 5-gate fail-fast suite on every detected git commit (Claude Code sessions in this repo only)

Mirror byte-parity for .agents/skills + plugins/karta + .codex/agents + bundled agent references, both via --check gates and re-verified inside validate_plugin using the same imported projection functions (writer and validator cannot disagree by construction, validate_plugin.py:22-24)
marketplace.json must enumerate exactly the skill dirs present, both directions
Claude/Codex plugin.json name+version parity; agent effort restricted to the shared {low,medium,high,xhigh} vocabulary; sandbox_mode derived from tools, never hand-set
All 6 hook scripts' embedded --self-tests executed on every validate_plugin run; unreferenced hook scripts and missing exec bits are errors
doc-gardner/kaizen opt-in config shape (enabled:bool, keys ⊆ {enabled,focus})
minimalism.md shadow byte-identity
prose_only_rules: Checklist FIDELITY: which packs resolve, overlay-over-builtin precedence, normalization into item lists, and BLOCKED-on-unresolvable-pin all live in karta-verify SKILL prose (SKILL.md:75); the hook checks only that something checklist-shaped is present
Running sync_codex_skills/sync_codex_agents in WRITE mode after a canonical edit — only the check side is enforced; the fix command is a prose suggestion in the drift output
KARTA_SKIP_GATE=1 usage policy ('intentional partial commits', docs/how-to/hooks.md:42-48) — the hatch is available to the agent itself in command text with no audit trail

All file mutation discipline via Bash: no guard matches Bash for binder/pack/kaizen writes — spec explicitly defers 'any hook that inspects Bash command internals' (2026-07-06-hooks-phase1-design.md:52)
The entire Codex runtime: every hook rule is doctrine + OS sandbox + push-only execpolicy there (docs/how-to/hooks.md:52); .codex/rules/karta.rules guards push, not commit or writes
Stop-gate compliance after the first nudge: 'an identical stop will pass' by design — the archive/merge fix itself is skill prose
Hook override/disable: any higher settings layer can disable a plugin hook (docs/how-to/hooks.md:36) — enforcement is opt-out-able by construction
External skill trust: skills-lock.json computedHash is never recomputed against .agents/skills content (sync_codex_skills.py:64-71 checks field presence only)

perf.context_cost: Near-zero standing cost — the subsystem's defining perf property. No SKILL.md; scripts run out-of-band. Live-session tokens: inject_karta_status ≤10 lines per SessionStart in binder-bearing repos; a deny costs one paragraph of stderr; a precommit block relays up to 40 gate-output lines (TAIL_LINES, precommit_gate.py:35). docs/how-to/hooks.md is 54 lines of human doc, never session-loaded.
perf.agent_spawns: 0 per use — every guard and gate is a python subprocess; the subsystem never spawns an agent. It does fork: up to 3 sequential guard processes per Write/Edit (binder + pack + confinement matcher groups in hooks.json), each a fresh python interpreter, pack guard adding a nested validator subprocess; Stop adds one process with ~5-15 git subprocesses.
serialization_points: Every git commit in the repo serially runs 5 gates (fail-fast); measured validate_plugin alone ≈2.4s because it re-executes all 6 hook --self-tests live, including guard_delivery_stop's fixture suite that builds ~15 temp git repos (validate_plugin.py:306-317) — paid on every commit even when hooks/ is untouched

Gates 2+3 (sync --check) are strict subsets of gate 4 (validate_plugin imports the same modules and re-runs the same projection comparisons), so the ~219 mirror files are byte-read and compared twice per commit
Three PreToolUse guard processes run sequentially on every Write/Edit anywhere (non-matching paths exit fast, but interpreter startup is per-guard, per-call)
redundant_work: Mirror byte-parity computed twice per commit (sync --check gates then validate_plugin sections 3-4)
validate_packs runs up to 3x on one pack change: PreToolUse Write deny-check, PostToolUse disk re-check, then the commit-gate pack lane
Hook self-tests re-run on every commit regardless of whether hooks/scripts changed

canonical skills/*/references copies of _shared files are themselves a deliberate 10-physical-copies-per-pack redundancy (spec line 37) that the whole check_shared_copies + sync machinery exists to police

dark_areas.area: Bash is a wide-open bypass of every plugin write guard
dark_areas.why_risky: binder immutability, pack validation, and kaizen confinement all match only Write|Edit|NotebookEdit (plus Task|Agent); `bash -c 'cat > .karta/binders/x.json'`, sed -i on a pack, or git checkout -- of a binder is invisible to all of them. The doc's headline claim 'rules the agent cannot skip' holds only for the file-tool surface
dark_areas.evidence: hooks/hooks.json matchers lines 6/16/26/36; docs/specs/2026-07-06-hooks-phase1-design.md:52 explicitly defers 'any hook that inspects Bash command internals beyond the commit-gate's simple parse'

dark_areas.area: sync_codex_skills write mode can rmtree an externally-installed skill on a degraded lock entry
dark_areas.why_risky: an .agents/skills/<name> dir whose skills-lock.json entry is missing or lacks ANY of the 4 required string fields is reclassified as karta-managed, hence an orphan, hence deleted wholesale in write mode — a hand-edited or format-migrated lockfile silently destroys installed third-party skill content
dark_areas.evidence: sync_codex_skills.py:64-71 (all LOCK_FIELDS must be non-empty strings), :74-77 (external = locked − canonical), :223-225 (`shutil.rmtree(MIRROR / name)` for orphan_dirs)

dark_areas.area: auditor-dispatch evidence check is a text heuristic an LLM satisfies trivially
dark_areas.why_risky: the FAIL-CLOSED guard's actual bar is 'the word checklist followed anywhere by one token matching [a-z]+.[0-9]+' — the fidelity of what travels to the safety-auditor (right packs, all packs, current rule text) rests entirely on karta-verify prose; the guard converts 'forgot checklists entirely' into a hard stop but cannot catch 'sent the wrong ones'
dark_areas.evidence: guard_auditor_dispatch.py:41-45 (`idx = text.lower().find("checklist"); return idx != -1 and bool(ID_TOKEN_RE.search(text[idx:]))`); self-test case line 131-132 passes on the bare string 'Resolved checklist: min.1 min.4 py.2 py.3'

dark_areas.area: commit-gate detection and escape hatch are equally available to the agent being gated
dark_areas.why_risky: detection is textual over the Bash command only: git aliases (git ci), a Makefile/script that commits internally, or any non-Claude-Code commit (plain terminal, Codex session) run zero gates — there is no CI backstop. And KARTA_SKIP_GATE=1 is honored from the command text itself, so the same model the gate constrains can legally prefix it; nothing logs or audits hatch use
dark_areas.evidence: precommit_gate.py:44 (_COMMIT_RE), :116 (`if f"{SKIP_VAR}=1" in command`); docs/how-to/hooks.md:17 scopes the gate to 'Claude Code (this repo's project settings)'; .codex/rules/karta.rules covers only git push

dark_areas.area: PostToolUse pack correction is advisory — a broken pack can persist on disk
dark_areas.why_risky: an Edit that breaks a pack lands before validation; exit 2 only feeds stderr back to the model. If the session ends or the model ploughs on, the malformed pack sits in the working tree, and the stated consequence ('silently drops out of plan-time matching and audit-time checklists') is live until the repo's own commit gate — which only exists in the karta dev repo, not in user projects
dark_areas.evidence: guard_pack_write.py:63-64 ('an Edit delta has no full content to validate; PostToolUse covers it'), :83-93 (validate-on-disk, no revert); user projects have no precommit pack lane

dark_areas.area: validate_plugin accepts any hooks.json event/matcher vocabulary
dark_areas.why_risky: _check_hooks iterates whatever event keys exist and only validates command tokens — a typo'd event name ('PreTooluse') or matcher ('Wrlte') validates green and the guard silently never fires; the orphan-script check would not catch it because the script is still referenced
dark_areas.evidence: validate_plugin.py:278 `for event, groups in (data.get("hooks") or {}).items():` — no whitelist of event names or matcher tool names anywhere in the function (270-317)

dark_areas.area: byte-parity mirrors ignore file mode; projections can lose exec bits
dark_areas.why_risky: expected()/projection_drift compare read_bytes only, and write mode recreates files via write_bytes (default 0644); a canonical skill script's +x does not travel to .agents/skills or plugins/karta, and only hooks/scripts get an os.access X_OK check — a silently-wrong class for any projection consumer that execs scripts directly
dark_areas.evidence: sync_codex_skills.py:143-152 (byte compare only), :236-246 (write_bytes, no chmod); validate_plugin.py:304 (X_OK checked only for hook manifest scripts)

dark_areas.area: Stop-gate's fail-open surface plus block-once makes it purely advisory in several real states
dark_areas.why_risky: missing session_id/cwd, non-git cwd, malformed binder, unresolvable sentinel, subagent-shaped payload, and stop_hook_active all pass silently; the archive check omits refs/remotes/*/karta/<slug>/integration so an integration branch that was pushed then locally deleted reads as complete-unarchived (spurious nudge) — and conversely one nudge per state is the entire enforcement budget
dark_areas.evidence: guard_delivery_stop.py:193-207 and 229-231 (fail-open exits), :144 (revs list has no remote-tracking integration ref); design intent 'a nudge, not a wall' in module docstring lines 26-28

dark_areas.area: binder immutability checks HEAD only and trusts the file-tool path string
dark_areas.why_risky: a binder staged (git add) but not yet committed is freely editable mid-plan (ls-tree HEAD misses the index); and the guard resolves the path as the tool call names it — it never resolves symlinks in intermediate components, so a symlinked alias dir to .karta/binders/ that doesn't textually match BINDER_RE would pass (path.resolve() happens only inside _tracked_in_head, after the regex gate)
dark_areas.evidence: guard_binder_immutability.py:48-50 (ls-tree HEAD), :59 (BINDER_RE.search on the raw target string before any resolution)

dark_areas.area: check_shared_copies verifies only what was actually copied
dark_areas.why_risky: it compares existing skill-side copies against _shared and requires completeness only for mirrored SUBdirs (a skill that never created references/sme/ owes nothing); top-level _shared files and non-.md assets are outside the check — so 'which skill must carry which shared file' is prose, and a forgotten first copy is invisible forever
dark_areas.evidence: check_shared_copies.py:27-29 (drift only `if rel in shared`), :33-38 (completeness gated on `(refs / sub.name).is_dir()`), :19 (`rglob("*.md")` only)

gaps.gap: No enforcement path at all outside Claude Code sessions: Codex/terminal commits skip the entire gate suite (no CI), and all six plugin guards are Claude-Code-only by design
gaps.impact: the repo's mirror-parity invariant and pack validity are only as strong as 'the contributor used Claude Code'; a terminal `git commit` can land drifted mirrors on main and nothing detects it until the next Claude Code commit attempt
gaps.evidence: docs/how-to/hooks.md:17-20 ('Not shipped in the plugin' / runtime column); spec 2026-07-06:37 acknowledges 'no CI, no hook' was the prior regime — the replacement is a local hook only

gaps.gap: skills-lock.json computedHash is dead weight: recorded but never recomputed against the mirrored external skill content
gaps.impact: tampered or drifted external skills in .agents/skills pass every gate; the lock only shields names from deletion, providing zero integrity of content
gaps.evidence: sync_codex_skills.py:53-71 — the only consumer of the lock checks field presence/type; grep shows no hash computation anywhere in scripts/

gaps.gap: Kaizen shadow byte-identity covers exactly one hard-coded pack
gaps.impact: any other built-in pack seeded to .karta/sme (kaizen seeds 'every pack the project uses') can drift from its canonical skills/_shared/sme source with only syntax validated — the exact drift class the minimalism check was built to stop
gaps.evidence: validate_plugin.py:104-106 hard-codes .karta/sme/minimalism.md; agents/karta-kaizen.md 'Seed on the first enabled run' section mandates seeding every resolved pack

gaps.gap: guard_pack_write's PostToolUse matcher omits NotebookEdit and its PreToolUse lane omits Edit; guard scope is asymmetric with the binder/confinement guards
gaps.impact: an Edit-created invalid pack exists on disk until corrective feedback lands (or forever, in a user project); coverage asymmetry across the three write guards is undocumented in hooks.md, which states 'A Write ... is checked ... before it lands' without the Edit caveat
gaps.evidence: hooks/hooks.json:16 (PreToolUse matcher 'Write' only) vs :48 (PostToolUse 'Edit|Write'); docs/how-to/hooks.md:12

gaps.gap: Stop-gate ref vocabulary tracks {built,done,failed} but the skills also define an 'accepted' ref namespace the guard never reads
gaps.impact: benign today (accept-waived items still get a done ref after the orchestrator merges), but the guard and the skill ref contract are maintained in two places with no shared source; a future ref-state addition (like 'accepted' was) silently changes stop-gate semantics only if someone remembers to update REF_STATES
gaps.evidence: guard_delivery_stop.py:41 REF_STATES tuple vs skills/karta-build/SKILL.md:418,435 ('never write the accepted namespace ... that ref is the orchestrator's')

gaps.gap: precommit gate's ROOT is the karta checkout that hosts the hook, regardless of which repo the commit targets
gaps.impact: `git -C /other/repo commit` from a karta session runs karta's gates against karta's tree (false positive, escape-hatch noise); conversely a karta commit issued from a session whose project dir is elsewhere runs nothing
gaps.evidence: precommit_gate.py:30 `ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve()...`; :88 `cwd=ROOT` for every gate regardless of payload cwd; .claude/settings.json binds via ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}

gaps.gap: inject_karta_status swallows all failure silently while shelling to a second script with a 15s timeout on every session start
gaps.impact: a hung or broken karta_next.py costs up to 15s of session-start latency with no error surfaced, and a degraded summary is indistinguishable from a correct one
gaps.evidence: inject_karta_status.py:63-70 (subprocess timeout=15, every exception → None), :185-187 (blanket except: pass)

contracts: Hook I/O contract: payload JSON on stdin; exit 0 allows, exit 2 blocks (PreToolUse/Stop) or feeds corrective stderr (PostToolUse); reason on stderr prefixed 'karta: ' (hooks/hooks.json description; all six guards)

Ref namespace: refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/{built,done,failed} written per karta-build/karta-deliver doctrine; guard_delivery_stop.REF_STATES consumes exactly these three (the skills' 'accepted' namespace is outside the guard's vocabulary)
Branch naming: refs/heads/karta/<slug>/integration is one of the three places an archive commit counts (guard_delivery_stop.py:144)

Binder paths: .karta/binders/<slug>.json live, .karta/binders/archive/<slug>.json delivered — shared by BINDER_RE (immutability), BINDER_PATH_RE (auditor dispatch), _live_slugs/_archive_committed (stop gate), and inject_karta_status glob
Binder shape consumed by hooks: top-level sme[] (list of pack-id strings) and work_items[].id (strings) — auditor guard and stop gate fail open/closed on malformation per their polarity

Pack rule-id grammar: '- [ ] <prefix>.<n> — <text>' item lines and registered PREFIXES — validate_packs.py is the schema; guard_auditor_dispatch.ITEM_LINE_RE deliberately mirrors it (comment at line 31)

Agent identity fields: hook payloads carry top-level agent_type/agent_id (verified against live harness); confinement matches agent_type exactly 'karta-kaizen' or '*:karta-kaizen'; auditor recognition is substring 'karta-safety-auditor' over subagent_type/agent_type/agent/agent_name/name of tool_input
CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT resolves plugin-internal scripts (validate_packs.py, karta_next.py) with a fallback to the guard's own tree; CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR anchors the repo precommit hook
Stop-gate sentinel: <git-common-dir>/karta-stop-gate.json, session_id→findings-fingerprint map, max 20 sessions, atomic replace; stop_hook_active honored as the harness loop guard
KARTA_SKIP_GATE=1 (command text or env) — the sole documented commit-gate escape hatch
skills-lock.json entry contract: {source, sourceType, skillPath, computedHash} all non-empty strings = protected external skill in .agents/skills

Projection surfaces the sync machinery owns and validate_plugin re-verifies: .agents/skills/<name>/ mirror, plugins/karta/{skills,.codex-plugin}/ install projection, .codex/agents/<name>.toml, skills/<BUNDLE_SITE[name]>/references/<name>.agent.md bundles

Agent frontmatter contract: name/description/codex_model required; effort ∈ {low, medium, high, xhigh} (shared Claude+Codex vocabulary — no Claude-only 'max', no Codex-only 'minimal'); sandbox_mode derived from tools (Write|Edit → workspace-write, else read-only)

Verdict vocabulary touched: the auditor gate ecosystem's BLOCKED/VIOLATION states (karta-verify SKILL) are what the dispatch guard's fail-closed denial protects; stop-gate messages name deliver:archive / karta-build 9c-single as the fix steps

Marketplace contracts: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json plugin source './' and exact skill enumeration; .agents/plugins/marketplace.json source.path './plugins/<name>'; Claude/Codex plugin.json name+version equality

subsystems.deliverable: Two things reach a karta user: (1) the static story — README, docs/how-to/*, docs/conventions/phase-labels.md — that a new user reads to install karta, trust its guarantees, and discover its skills; and (2) the doc-gardner mechanism — an opt-in per-repo switch (.karta/doc-gardner.json) that makes every karta-deliver run end by rewriting the user's drifted prose docs to match the delivered code as one revertible `docs: gardner <slug>` commit, with the karta-plainlanguage skill as the prose standard everything karta shows a human is held to.

flow.step: User opts in by committing .karta/doc-gardner.json {"enabled": true, "focus"?}

dark_areas.area: The gardner agent cannot compute its own blast radius: instructed to use a shell it does not have

The dispatching skill passes the range string, not the file list (SKILL.md:39). On Claude Code the registered subagent therefore has no specified way to derive the blast radius; stale-description and coverage-gap detection (drift kinds 2 and 5, both blast-radius-scoped) silently degrade, and the spec never says what the agent does when git diff is impossible.

Directly against the owner's determinism preference.

The delivery Stop-gate — the hook built exactly to catch a delivery ending dirty — checks merge and archive state only; a delivery that never ran the gardner phase stops clean.

Same pattern for kaizen how-to:21 ('the plugin validator schema-checks the file when your repo commits one') — validate_plugin.py's ROOT is the karta repo, not 'your repo'.

the four repo gates run in nearly every oracle: validate_plugin.py --self-test, check_shared_copies.py --self-test, sync_codex_agents.py --check, sync_codex_skills.py --check

six Claude Code hooks; evidence of live firing: guard_auditor_dispatch blocked a real dispatch (dogfood Finding 2), guard_writer_confinement blocked a live out-of-surface kaizen write through the installed plugin (v1.19.0-checklist.md line 23), guard_delivery_stop 29/29 fixture repos (v2.21.0-checklist.md line 10)
git refs/tags as the resume record: refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/{built,done}, wave-N/wave-N-base tags
prose_only_rules: merge-queue completion and binder archival were prose until 2.21.0 — the Stop-gate spec's own justification: 'an orchestrator under context pressure can end its turn having declared victory with built refs still standing... today nothing below the agent catches either' (delivery-stop-gate design, line 7); and even now the gate is a one-shot fail-open nudge, not a wall
release-checklist bookkeeping: nothing forces test-drive boxes to be ticked after the run — v2.21.0's test-drive section remains fully unchecked post-pass
the karta/<slug>/delivered tag convention exists in no spec and was applied to 3 of 4 tag-bearing deliveries then dropped

gate-economy downgrades are discretionary prose: dogfood waves 2-3 used 'a single safety vote per item and dispositioned acceptance from the green self-test rather than a separate reviewer agent, to keep the cost proportionate' (findings.md line 125) — an LLM chose to skip a gate agent and nothing recorded it machine-readably

Codex-side boundaries are instruction-enforced per the compatibility matrix itself (codex README lines 11-16: doc-gardner surface, kaizen path boundary, plannotator no-implicit-commit all 'instruction-enforced'; hooks 'Unavailable')

binder work-item contract is un-checkable prose: schema allows object|string|null and practice converged on monolithic ~5.5KB strings (delivery-stop-gate.json line 52) that a build worker must fully honor with no mechanical check of any individual clause

the README stated the shared-terms landed-item contract before the code implemented it — the 1.19.0 fix note: 'the README prose already states the fixed contract...this only makes the code match it' (shared-terms-pending.json line 16): docs-as-contract drifted from code and only a live false-halt exposed it

perf.context_cost: The evidence corpus itself: 6 archive binders total ~85KB (delivery-stop-gate.json 18KB for only 3 items — its one guard-item contract is a single ~5.5KB prose string, essentially the whole design spec re-inlined); checklists 30-42 lines each; findings.md 164 lines; codex README 30 lines; the four docs/plans files are 20-65KB each (pre-pivot superpowers-style plans, not loaded by live karta runs). A build worker on the stop-gate binder ingests 18KB of binder alone before reading any code.
perf.agent_spawns: Empirically per delivery: 1 orchestrator + 1 build worker per item + gate agents. Dogfood (7 items, 3 waves): 7 workers, wave 1 ran both gate agents, waves 2-3 dropped to one safety vote per item and no acceptance agent — the full-ceremony cost was explicitly judged disproportionate on a 7-item run. codex-119's three e2e items each spawned nested Codex sessions (read-only gate pairs, a workspace-write kaizen writer, browser annotation) — the most spawn-expensive binder recorded. Kaizen adds a post-delivery spawn that has been a no-op in every recorded run — a pure spawn tax so far.
serialization_points: serial FIFO merge queue with per-item oracle re-validation against the moving tip — by design, observed in all runs
wave 1 of the dogfood was deliberately a single item 'run inline, to prove the pipeline before scaling out' (findings.md line 40) — first-wave parallelism sacrificed for confidence
codex-119's dependency shape serialized everything behind fix-macos-binder-paths (all 3 test items depend on it), then publish depends on all 3 — a diamond with no wave wider than 3
Stop-gate block-once mechanics add one blocked-stop + continuation turn per dirty state
redundant_work: the same rules exist in up to 4 prose copies: design spec → binder contract (re-stating the spec nearly verbatim, cf. delivery-stop-gate.json item contract vs the design doc) → SKILL doctrine → release checklist narrative

validate_plugin.py --self-test appears in 22 of 23 item oracles and itself executes every hook's self-test — the full hook suite re-runs at every item gate, every merge re-validation, and every post-wave check

the codex-119 binder re-certified 1.14-1.16 checklist claims months after those releases because the original checklists asserted Codex behavior nobody had run — evidence recorded twice, once optimistically and once for real (21 boxes still open, 20 now closed with links)

dogfood ran the entire 7-item delivery twice (full clone, then blind history-truncated re-run) because the first run's workers could see the answer commit — a full duplicate delivery to get honest numbers

dark_areas.area: Ref/tag survival is inconsistent with doctrine
dark_areas.why_risky: karta-deliver SKILL.md line 199 asserts 'the delivered run's refs/karta/<slug>/ refs and wave tags remain in git' (and slug-shadow protection depends on that), but only 4 of 6 archived binders have surviving refs and kaizen-frame + codex-119-compatibility have neither refs nor tags — any consumer (status engine, stop-gate, slug-freshness warning) reading history for those two sees nothing
dark_areas.evidence: git for-each-ref 'refs/karta/**' lists refs only for cross-item-consistency, delivery-stop-gate, shared-terms-pending, writer-confinement; git tag -l 'karta/*' counts: cross-item-consistency 7, delivery-stop-gate 4, shared-terms-pending 5, writer-confinement 5, kaizen-frame 0, codex-119 0

dark_areas.area: Undocumented karta/<slug>/delivered tag convention
dark_areas.why_risky: three deliveries minted a tag no spec defines, the fourth stopped — orchestrator sessions invent and drop artifact conventions, so downstream tooling cannot rely on any tag beyond wave-N/wave-N-base
dark_areas.evidence: grep -r for the delivered tag across skills/, hooks/, scripts/ returns nothing; git tag shows karta/{cross-item-consistency,shared-terms-pending,writer-confinement}/delivered but no karta/delivery-stop-gate/delivered

dark_areas.area: Release evidence write-back has no enforcement
dark_areas.why_risky: the checklist is the certification artifact, but ticking it is manual and post-hoc; 2.21.0 shipped, its live test-drive passed (2026-07-16 per project memory), and the file's test-drive boxes are all still '[ ]' with no later commit touching it — the repo's own evidence trail says 2.21.0 is uncertified
dark_areas.evidence: docs/releases/v2.21.0-checklist.md lines 16-30 all unchecked; git log -- docs/releases/v2.21.0-checklist.md shows only 7ae1245 (the version bump)

dark_areas.area: e2e oracles cannot mechanically verify what they certify
dark_areas.why_risky: codex-119's three e2e items' oracle commands are 'codex --version && <repo gates>' (or plannotator --version) — the executable part proves tool presence and repo integrity, not the e2e behavior; the real acceptance lived in human-read evidence files, and two items closed only via human waivers as accepted-done, one explicitly because 'transcript-backed assertions were not implemented as executable contract tests'
dark_areas.evidence: codex-119-compatibility.json lines 104, 141, 178 (oracle commands); docs/showcase/codex-1.19-compatibility/README.md lines 20-23 (the two waivers)

dark_areas.area: Stale evidence docs the gardner never touches
dark_areas.why_risky: docs/backlog/README.md opens with 'Current release: main is at v1.10.0' while main ships 2.21.0 — the backlog's framing (and its still-pending skill→agent conversion, memory-confirmed pending) sits under a header 11 releases stale; doc-gardner exists but is opt-in and delivery-triggered, and no delivery listed this file in touches
dark_areas.evidence: docs/backlog/README.md line 5 vs .claude-plugin manifest at 2.21.0 (commit 7ae1245)

dark_areas.area: Contract-field shape drifted to unbounded prose
dark_areas.why_risky: the schema allows object|string|null for a work-item contract; early binders used structured objects (exposes/preserves/fail_closed_cases keys), the last four use monolithic strings up to ~5.5KB — nothing can check a single clause of them, and their size dominates binder context cost
dark_areas.evidence: binder-schema.json $defs/workItem/properties/contract: {"type": ["object","string","null"]}; kaizen-frame.json line 47 (object) vs delivery-stop-gate.json line 52 (one giant string)

gaps.gap: The 'integration' oracle type has never been used: across all 23 archived work items the distribution is smoke 15, unit 5, e2e 3, integration 0 — and most smoke oracles are the identical repo-gates command, so 'acceptance' for doc/skill items usually means 'the repo integrity suite passes' plus prose assertions an agent eyeballs
gaps.impact: the verify vocabulary (unit/integration/e2e/smoke) overstates the framework's discriminating power; item-specific behavioral verification is concentrated in the 5 unit oracles (the self-test scripts)
gaps.evidence: oracle.type fields across the 6 files in .karta/binders/archive/; e.g. writer-confinement.json items 2-4 all smoke with command 'uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test'

gaps.gap: The retry/halt machinery is fixture-proven but production-unexercised: no failed ref exists anywhere in repo history, real deliveries logged 1 kickback total (1.18.0) and 'zero kickbacks' (both 1.19.0 binders, 3/3 clean-done for 2.21.0), and revert-the-wave has only ever run against a throwaway fixture repo
gaps.impact: the most complex prose in karta-deliver (four-way choice, revert-the-wave ref surgery, retry-exhaustion escalation) has near-zero empirical validation on real work; its first real-world execution will be its test
gaps.evidence: git for-each-ref shows only built/done refs; v1.19.0-checklist.md line 17; v2.21.0-checklist.md line 11; v1.18.0-checklist.md line 22 (fixture-only wave revert)

gaps.gap: Every hand-off that lived only as prose eventually slipped in a real run: cross-item wording drifted between unlinked items (blind dogfood), the shared-terms code contradicted its documented contract and false-halted a live wave, and the merge/archive hand-offs worried the designers enough to build the Stop-gate — but the two newest below-the-agent protections are asymmetric (Stop-gate is one-shot fail-open; Codex has none of the six hooks)
gaps.impact: the evidence supports the owner's determinism thesis strongly — prose rules decay under context pressure — while showing the remaining prose surface (Codex entirely, delivery-end after the single nudge, SubagentStop whiff-workers deferred) is where the next slippage will occur
gaps.evidence: findings.md lines 113-118; v1.19.0-checklist.md line 8 ('this actually happened'); delivery-stop-gate design line 7 and out-of-scope line 69 (SubagentStop deferral); codex README line 16 (hooks Unavailable)

gaps.gap: Cross-item consistency enforcement depends on plan-time human declaration: check_shared_terms only checks declared entries, surfacing candidates is 'best-effort, not a guarantee', and the dogfood-found drift class recurs silently for any term nobody declared
gaps.impact: the flagship dogfood finding is only partially closed — deterministic where declared, blind elsewhere
gaps.evidence: cross-item-consistency.json line 110; v1.18.0-checklist.md line 10 ('The check catches only declared terms — an inherent limit')

gaps.gap: Dogfood fidelity numbers need the blind caveat attached: the first run's two byte-identical files came from workers reading the answer commit in history; only the blind re-run (no byte-identical files, +37% to +190% larger diffs, more self-tests than the human: 18/16/34/40 vs 15/14/30/35) is honest — and the blind result was never fully gate-priced (waves 2-3 ran reduced gates)
gaps.impact: any synthesis quoting 'karta reproduced the human's work byte-identically' is quoting the contaminated run
gaps.evidence: findings.md lines 93-111 and 124-126

gaps.gap: Wave-level gate downgrades and waivers exist only in narrative prose (findings.md limitations, showcase README waiver section) — no binder field, ref, or ledger records that an item's acceptance was self-test-dispositioned or human-waived
gaps.impact: an archived binder's clean appearance is indistinguishable from a full-ceremony pass; accepted-done vs clean-done survives only in the deliver-session's tags/refs (which for codex-119 did not survive at all)
gaps.evidence: findings.md line 125; codex README lines 20-23; no waiver/disposition field in binder-schema.json

contracts: refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/{built,done,failed,accepted} — item state refs; built==done sha on clean fast merges (observed)
tags karta/<slug>/wave-<N> and karta/<slug>/wave-<N>-base (doctrine) plus the undocumented karta/<slug>/delivered (3 of 4 recent deliveries)
branches karta/<slug>/integration and karta/<slug>/item-<id>

commit labels: [karta:item-<id>] on item commits; 'plan(karta): ...'/'chore(karta): plan <slug> binder' for plan commits; 'chore(karta): archive binder <slug> — delivered' for archival; 'kaizen:' label reserved for pack edits (never yet emitted)
binder paths .karta/binders/<slug>.json → .karta/binders/archive/<slug>.json (git mv, content-immutable, hook-guarded)

binder fields observed in practice: sme[] pins, shared_terms[{id,canonical,items}], oracle.type ∈ {unit,integration,e2e,smoke} (integration unused), estimate ∈ {S,M,L}, contract: object|string|null, surface.flagged+signals, env_contract/runtime_contract, design_facts.source (nullable)
verdict vocabulary in evidence: safety PASS/BLOCKED, acceptance CONFORMANT, SHARED TERMS: OK/DRIFT, [PENDING]/[PASS]/[FAIL] + 'N/N checks passed', clean-done vs accepted-done (waiver)
the four repo gates as the universal oracle backbone: uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test · check_shared_copies.py --self-test · sync_codex_agents.py --check · sync_codex_skills.py --check
Stop-gate sentinel <git-common-dir>/karta-stop-gate.json (session_id → state fingerprint, pruned to 20)

evidence file conventions: docs/releases/v<X>-checklist.md (pre-flight + test-drive checkboxes), docs/showcase/<topic>/ (findings/evidence, source-of-truth rule: summaries must not exceed the evidence files), docs/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<name>-design.md feeding binder design_facts.source

3-way mirror discipline appearing in every binder's touches: skills/ → .agents/skills/ → plugins/karta/skills/ via sync_codex_skills.py; agents/<name>.md → .codex/agents/*.toml + references/<name>.agent.md via sync_codex_agents.py
